# TEST ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

## Progress

| Demo | Mode(s) covered | Status |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | M1 CSR insn match + M8 end-of-program | done |
| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | done |
| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | done |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | done |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume + M3 icount exit | done |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | done |
| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | done |
| I1 | `demo.sh` runner + `make demo` target | done |
| I2 | CTest `switching-mode` label registration | done |
| I3 | `run_e2e.sh` integration | done |

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain
  -> vsim `--hybrid-icount 5000` -> drain. Both PCs verified inside the
  RVC loop body (0x100002 / 0x100004). QEMU2 handback is intentionally
  skipped: icount_spin has no natural semihosting exit, and using
  `--qemu-binary` would hang. M8 is covered by T1 / T2 / T6.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`
- Dependencies: F5 (plugin icount), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
- Pass criterion: both QEMU1 and vsim exit 200 with state files whose PC
  lies inside the icount_spin loop.

### T4 - demo_bbv_profile.sh (M4)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M`; runs `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
  on the resulting `.bb` file; parses simpts + weights. Outputs persist
  under `$WORKDIR` so T5/T7 can consume them.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh`
- Dependencies: F7 (BBV emit), F12 (simpoint binary).
- Pass criterion: simpoint exits 0; non-empty simpts + weights; prints chosen
  slice indices and weight sum (informational).

### T5 - demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh (M5 + M3)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Demonstrates trigger-mode mixing on a CSR-less workload.
  Entry via plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K` (M5); exit via vsim `--hybrid-icount`
  (M3). Reads simpts from `$SIMPTS` env if set (T4 output); else uses
  `SLICE_AT` env. M8 is covered by T1/T2/T6; icount_spin has no natural
  semihosting exit.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`
- Dependencies: F11 (plugin slice-consume), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
  T4 optional for SIMPTS env.
- Pass criterion: QEMU drains at slice boundary (idx=K+1 target=K, pc in
  loop); vsim drains after VSIM_ICOUNT insns (pc in loop); both exit 200.

### T6 - demo_archive_replay.sh (M6 + M7)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Inlines a one-step plan YAML (`qemu / kind: csr / on_exit:
  archive_as: snap/0`), runs `orchestrator run --archive-root $WORKDIR`,
  validates `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` (schema=1, abi_magic,
  mem_size=128MB), then `orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/0
  --resume-with vsim`. Verifies replay PC advanced past archive PC.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh`
- Dependencies: F9 (archive emit), F10 (replay).
- Pass criterion: both orchestrator invocations exit cleanly; meta.json
  validates; replay PC differs from archive PC.

### T7 - demo_simpoint_loop.sh (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7, centerpiece)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Full production SimPoint pipeline on rt_c_v_regs.
  (1) Profile: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=20,icount=2000 -> 15 BBV rows.
  (2) Cluster: simpoint -maxK 2 -dim 2 -> 2 cluster representatives.
  (3) Per phase: QEMU+plugin slice=20,slice_at=K -> drain; archive via
      `orchestrator.checkpoint.write_archive` Python helper into
      $WORKDIR/snap/K/.
  (4) Per archive: `orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim`
      hydrates the mmap, vsim drains naturally on csrwi 0x7C0,1.
  Defaults baked for rt_c_v_regs (SLICE=20, ICOUNT=2000); other fixtures
  may need different env overrides.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh`
- Dependencies: T4, T5, T6 (this exercises everything they exercise).
  Implementation note: skipped a per-phase YAML template; the
  orchestrator's drain argv builder does not yet thread slice/slice_at,
  so per-phase QEMU drains are direct invocations and archive writes
  use the Python helper directly. Orchestrator IS used for replay.
- Pass criterion: K phase archives created with valid state.bin + mem +
  meta.json; K replays exit 0; final tally line printed.

## Final Integration

### I1 - demo.sh runner + make demo target

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: User-facing entry point. `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh`
  walks T1..T7 sequentially, prints per-mode banners, and emits a
  pass/fail tally. Honors `FILTER=<regex>` to subset. Makefile gets a
  `demo` phony target that runs `all` then `demo.sh`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh`
  - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` (add `demo` target)

### I2 - CTest switching-mode label registration

- Status: done
- Description: `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach added to
  `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` (mirrors the `VSIM_RT_FIXTURES`
  pattern at lines 128-170). Seven CTest entries with labels
  `hybrid-e2e;switching-mode` and per-demo COST (T1/T2/T4 = 30s,
  T3/T5/T6 = 60s, T7 = 180s). `ctest -L switching-mode` runs the
  suite; takes effect on the next `build_vsim.sh` reconfigure.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`

### I3 - run_e2e.sh integration

- Status: done
- Description: Added a `DEMO_TESTS` array next to `NEG_TESTS` in
  `run_e2e.sh`, with one entry per demo. Demos run once each (no
  csr/pc loop because each script hard-codes its trigger). Workplan
  appends `demo|name|cmd` tuples; summary line gained a `demo` column.
  `icount_spin` added to `REQUIRED_ELFS` (used by T3/T4/T5/T7).
  Smoke-tested: `FILTER=demo_csr_oneshot bash run_e2e.sh` runs T1 and
  reports `demo 1p/0f/0s`.
- Key files:
  - `run_e2e.sh`

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Switching-mode demo runner: sequentially exercise every M1-M8 mode
# end-to-end via the labeled demo scripts under
# hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_*.sh.
#
# Each demo prints its own banner + per-mode PASS/FAIL line. This
# wrapper adds a final tally and propagates a non-zero exit if any
# demo fails. SKIPs (artifacts missing) do not fail the overall run.
#
# Env (auto-detected from repo layout if unset):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   FILTER        regex; only run demos whose name matches
#
# Usage:
#   bash demo.sh                       # all demos
#   FILTER=simpoint bash demo.sh       # just T4 + T7
#   FILTER='csr|pc' bash demo.sh       # T1 + T2

set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
VSIM_DIR=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$VSIM_DIR/.." && pwd)
DEMO_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"

export VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$TOP_ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
export VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$TOP_ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
export VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$VSIM_DIR/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
FILTER="${FILTER:-}"

# Ordered demo list. Each entry: <id>|<demo-script>
DEMOS=(
  "T1|demo_csr_oneshot.sh"
  "T2|demo_pc_oneshot.sh"
  "T3|demo_icount_roundtrip.sh"
  "T4|demo_bbv_profile.sh"
  "T5|demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh"
  "T6|demo_archive_replay.sh"
  "T7|demo_simpoint_loop.sh"
)

cat <<EOF
============================================================
Hybrid Sim Switching-Mode Demo Suite
============================================================
Repo root : $TOP_ROOT
QEMU      : $VSIM_QEMU
Plugin    : $VSIM_PLUGIN
vsim      : $VSIM_BIN
Filter    : ${FILTER:-<none>}
============================================================
EOF

PASS=0; FAIL=0; SKIP=0
declare -a RESULTS=()
START_TS=$(date +%s)

for entry in "${DEMOS[@]}"; do
  IFS="|" read -r ID SCRIPT <<<"$entry"
  if [ -n "$FILTER" ] && ! echo "$SCRIPT" | grep -qE "$FILTER"; then
    continue
  fi
  printf "\n--- %s : %s ---\n" "$ID" "$SCRIPT"
  if [ ! -x "$DEMO_DIR/$SCRIPT" ]; then
    printf "  MISSING: %s\n" "$DEMO_DIR/$SCRIPT"
    RESULTS+=("$ID  $SCRIPT  MISSING")
    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
    continue
  fi
  bash "$DEMO_DIR/$SCRIPT"
  RC=$?
  case "$RC" in
    0)  PASS=$((PASS + 1)); RESULTS+=("$ID  $SCRIPT  PASS") ;;
    77) SKIP=$((SKIP + 1)); RESULTS+=("$ID  $SCRIPT  SKIP") ;;
    *)  FAIL=$((FAIL + 1)); RESULTS+=("$ID  $SCRIPT  FAIL(rc=$RC)") ;;
  esac
done

END_TS=$(date +%s)
ELAPSED=$((END_TS - START_TS))

printf "\n============================================================\n"
printf "Demo Suite Summary  (elapsed %ds)\n" "$ELAPSED"
printf "============================================================\n"
for line in "${RESULTS[@]}"; do
  printf "  %s\n" "$line"
done
printf "  ----\n"
printf "  PASS=%d  FAIL=%d  SKIP=%d\n" "$PASS" "$FAIL" "$SKIP"

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  exit 1
fi
exit 0

content.text:
# /loop — schedule a recurring or self-paced prompt

Parse the input below into `[interval] <prompt…>` and schedule it.

## Parsing (in priority order)

1. **Leading token**: if the first whitespace-delimited token matches `^\d+[smhd]$` (e.g. `5m`, `2h`), that's the interval; the rest is the prompt.
2. **Trailing "every" clause**: otherwise, if the input ends with `every <N><unit>` or `every <N> <unit-word>` (e.g. `every 20m`, `every 5 minutes`, `every 2 hours`), extract that as the interval and strip it from the prompt. Only match when what follows "every" is a time expression — `check every PR` has no interval.
3. **No interval**: otherwise, the entire input is the prompt and you'll self-pace dynamically (see "Dynamic mode" below).

If the resulting prompt is empty, show usage `/loop [interval] <prompt>` and stop.

Examples:
- `5m /babysit-prs` → interval `5m`, prompt `/babysit-prs` (rule 1)
- `check the deploy every 20m` → interval `20m`, prompt `check the deploy` (rule 2)
- `run tests every 5 minutes` → interval `5m`, prompt `run tests` (rule 2)
- `check the deploy` → no interval → dynamic mode, prompt `check the deploy` (rule 3)
- `check every PR` → no interval → dynamic mode, prompt `check every PR` (rule 3 — "every" not followed by time)
- `5m` → empty prompt → show usage

## Offer cloud first

Before any scheduling step, check whether EITHER is true:
- the parsed interval (rule 1 or 2) is **≥60 minutes**, or
- regardless of which rule matched, the original input uses daily phrasing ("every morning", "daily", "every day", "each night", "every weekday")

If either is true, call AskUserQuestion first:
- `question`: "This loop stops when you close this session. Set it up as a cloud schedule instead so it keeps running?"
- `header`: "Schedule"
- `options`: `[{label: "Cloud schedule (recommended)", description: "Runs in Anthropic's cloud even after you close this session"}, {label: "This session only", description: "Runs in this terminal until you exit"}]`

If they pick **Cloud schedule**: do NOT call CronCreate. Invoke the `schedule` skill directly via the Skill tool with `args` set to their original input verbatim (e.g. `Skill({skill: "schedule", args: "every morning tell me a joke"})`), then follow that skill's instructions to completion. Do NOT tell the user to run /schedule themselves. **Then stop — do not continue to any section below** (no CronCreate, no ScheduleWakeup, no "execute the prompt now").
If they pick **This session only**:
- If the trigger was a parsed ≥60-minute interval (rule 1 or 2): continue below with that interval.
- If the trigger was daily phrasing only (rule 3, no parsed interval): do NOT call CronCreate. Explain that a daily-cadence loop won't fire before this session closes, so there's nothing useful to schedule locally — suggest they either pick Cloud schedule, or re-run `/loop` with an explicit shorter interval (e.g. `/loop 1h <prompt>`) if they want a session loop. Then stop.
If neither trigger condition was met: continue below.

## Fixed-interval mode (rules 1 and 2)

Convert the interval to a cron expression:

| Interval pattern      | Cron expression     | Notes                                    |
|-----------------------|---------------------|------------------------------------------|
| `Nm` where N ≤ 59   | `*/N * * * *`     | every N minutes                          |
| `Nm` where N ≥ 60   | `0 */H * * *`     | round to hours (H = N/60, must divide 24)|
| `Nh` where N ≤ 23   | `0 */N * * *`     | every N hours                            |
| `Nd`                | `0 0 */N * *`     | every N days at midnight local           |
| `Ns`                | treat as `ceil(N/60)m` | cron minimum granularity is 1 minute  |

**If the interval doesn't cleanly divide its unit** (e.g. `7m` → `*/7 * * * *` gives uneven gaps at :56→:00; `90m` → 1.5h which cron can't express), pick the nearest clean interval and tell the user what you rounded to before scheduling.

Then:
1. Call CronCreate with: `cron` (the expression above), `prompt` (the parsed prompt verbatim), `recurring: true`.
2. Briefly confirm: what's scheduled, the cron expression, the human-readable cadence, that recurring tasks auto-expire after 7 days, and that the user can cancel sooner with CronDelete (include the job ID). Only if you did NOT show the cloud-offer AskUserQuestion above (i.e., neither trigger condition applied), end the confirmation with this exact line on its own, italicized: `_Runs until you close this session · For durable cloud-based loops, use /schedule_`. If the user already answered that question, omit this line.
3. **Then immediately execute the parsed prompt now** — don't wait for the first cron fire. If it's a slash command, invoke it via the Skill tool; otherwise act on it directly.

## Dynamic mode (rule 3 — no interval)

The user wants you to self-pace. Decide what makes the next iteration worth running — a passage of time, or an observable event.

1. **Run the parsed prompt now.** If it's a slash command, invoke it via the Skill tool; otherwise act on it directly.
2. **If the next run is gated on an event** (CI finishing, a log line matching, a file changing, a PR comment) and no Monitor is already running for it: arm one now with `persistent: true`. Its events arrive as `<task-notification>` messages and wake this loop immediately — you do not wait for the ScheduleWakeup deadline. Arm once; on later iterations call TaskList first and skip this step if a monitor is already running.
3. **At the end of this turn, call ScheduleWakeup** with:
   - `delaySeconds`: with a Monitor armed this is the **fallback heartbeat** — how long to wait if no event fires (lean 1200–1800s; idle ticks past the 5-minute cache window are pure overhead). Without a Monitor this is the cadence — pick based on what you observed. Read the tool's own description for cache-aware delay guidance.
   - `reason`: one short sentence on why you picked that delay.
   - `prompt`: the full original /loop input verbatim, prefixed with `/loop ` so the next firing re-enters this skill and continues the loop. For example, if the user typed `/loop check the deploy`, pass `/loop check the deploy` as the prompt.
4. **If you were woken by a `<task-notification>`** rather than this prompt: handle the event in the context of the loop task, then call ScheduleWakeup again with the same `prompt` and the same 1200–1800s `delaySeconds` from step 3 — the Monitor remains the wake signal; this only resets the safety net.
5. **To stop the loop**, omit the ScheduleWakeup call and TaskStop any Monitor you armed (use TaskList to find the task ID if it is no longer in context).
6. Briefly confirm: that you're self-pacing, whether a Monitor is the primary wake signal, that you ran the task now, and what fallback delay you picked.

## Input

5m read TODO.md and implement the feature with "Status: fix it"
Loading CronCreate to schedule the 5-minute loop.

1	# TODO: Failure Modes Backlog
2	
3	Prioritized list of conditions under which the hybrid QEMU+vsim simulator
4	will silently fail or hang on real workloads. Derived from a 2026-05-17
5	audit of `qemu_plugin/`, `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`,
6	`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`, and the test suite at
7	`hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/`.
8	
9	Format: one row per failure mode.
10	
11	- Trigger: the production scenario that exposes the bug
12	- Breaks: the observable symptom
13	- Source: file(s) where the gap lives
14	- Status: not-started | in-progress | done
15	
16	## Tier 1 - Will fail on the next real workload
17	
18	### F-T1.1 Semihosting inside ROI
19	
20	- Trigger: Any C fixture calling `printf`, `malloc`, `_exit`, or any
21	  libgloss-routed syscall between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()`.
22	- Breaks: libgloss_vh emits the semihosting magic triple
23	  (`slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`); vsim's trap handler
24	  `runtime/trap.S` falls into `handler.c` which calls `_exit(mcause)`,
25	  itself a semihosting ebreak. Infinite trap loop / test timeout.
26	- Source: `docs/vsim_semihosting_investigation.md` (unsolved);
27	  `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/{trap.S,handler.c}`
28	- Status: not-started
29	
30	### F-T1.2 FP state not restored to vsim
31	
32	- Trigger: Any workload using f0..f31 inside ROI.
33	- Breaks: QEMU1 plugin drains FPRs into `state.bin`. ResumeDriver does
34	  not write FPRs. vsim FPRs remain at RTL reset value. StateDrain
35	  passes through the stale QEMU1 snapshot to QEMU2 unchanged.
36	  FP computations in ROI silently corrupt register contents.
37	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`,
38	  `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`
39	- Status: fix it
40	
41	### F-T1.3 PMP state not restored to vsim
42	
43	- Trigger: Any workload depending on PMP-enforced memory regions
44	  inside ROI.
45	- Breaks: Plugin drains pmpcfg[0..15] and pmpaddr[0..63] but
46	  ResumeDriver writes none. vsim sees zeroed PMP entries; any
47	  write-protect or execute-lock setup from QEMU1 evaporates.
48	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`
49	- Status: not-started
50	
51	### F-T1.4 Exception state lost across vsim ROI
52	
53	- Trigger: Any trap (illegal-insn, page-fault, alignment, ebreak) fires
54	  inside ROI.
55	- Breaks: StateDrain returns the QEMU1 snapshot for mcause/mtval/mepc
56	  instead of vsim's current trap state. QEMU2 receives stale values;
57	  any post-handoff trap diagnostic logic is wrong.
58	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` (passthrough path)
59	- Status: not-started
60	
61	### F-T1.5 LR/SC reservation not transferred
62	
63	- Trigger: An lr.{w,d} retires before the trigger and the matching
64	  sc.{w,d} retires after - or any atomic across the handoff.
65	- Breaks: Reservation is not in the ABI struct, never round-trips.
66	  sc.* always fails on re-entry, so any `__atomic_compare_exchange`
67	  loop spins forever.
68	- Source: `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (missing field); resume/drain
69	  both
70	- Status: not-started
71	
72	## Tier 2 - Will fail when scaling
73	
74	### F-T2.1 priv mode hardcoded to M
75	
76	- Trigger: Linux/kernel workload running in S- or U-mode at the
77	  trigger boundary.
78	- Breaks: `state_drain.hpp:40` and `resume_driver.hpp:40` force
79	  `priv=3`; DCSR.prv never written. `dret` always resumes in M-mode.
80	  S/U code raises illegal-insn -> trap loop.
81	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`,
82	  `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`
83	- Status: not-started
84	
85	### F-T2.2 Workload exceeding 128MB mmap
86	
87	- Trigger: Heap, stack, or static data extending past the default 128MB
88	  shared mmap.
89	- Breaks: vsim's SimpleMemory bounds reject the access or silently
90	  truncate; QEMU sees MMIO instead of RAM. No startup-time bounds
91	  check on ELF segments against mmap size.
92	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/platform/simple_memory.hpp`,
93	  orchestrator mmap sizing
94	- Status: not-started
95	
96	### F-T2.3 Multi-hart resume path
97	
98	- Trigger: `_NDS_NHART=2` configuration; workload using hart 1, atomic
99	  fences, IPIs, or hart-to-hart sync.
100	- Breaks: ABI carries `hart_id` and per-hart arrays but ResumeDriver
101	  and StateDrain only handle hart 0. Resuming hart 1 from hart 0's
102	  state corrupts both.
103	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/{resume_driver,state_drain}.hpp`,
104	  `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (thread selection)
105	- Status: not-started
106	
107	### F-T2.4 mcycle/minstret monotonicity
108	
109	- Trigger: Long-running workload; or any consumer reading these CSRs
110	  across the handoff.
111	- Breaks: No test asserts monotonicity. If the icount trigger is off
112	  by one, vsim's mcycle could be less than QEMU1's exit mcycle and
113	  nothing would catch it.
114	- Source: gap in test suite; ABI does carry mcycle/minstret
115	- Status: not-started
116	
117	### F-T2.5 MMIO inside ROI
118	
119	- Trigger: Workload calls `putchar`, touches UART/PLIC, accesses ROM,
120	  or hits aliased memory inside ROI.
121	- Breaks: Phase-5 interconnect watch (`hybrid_plan.md` Section 5) was
122	  never implemented. Writes to MMIO ranges either vanish or hit
123	  SimpleMemory as if they were RAM. QEMU2 device state desyncs from
124	  vsim's view.
125	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/platform/interconnect.hpp` (no watch);
126	  `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5
127	- Status: not-started
128	
129	## Tier 3 - Bug surfaces invisible to current suite
130	
131	### F-T3.1 State-value opacity in M3-M7 demos
132	
133	- Trigger: Any bug that copies the wrong register, byte-swaps, or
134	  truncates a 64-bit value during M3 (icount), M4 (BBV), M5
135	  (slice-consume), M6 (archive), or M7 (replay).
136	- Breaks: Demos `demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`,
137	  `demo_bbv_profile.sh`, `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`,
138	  `demo_archive_replay.sh`, `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` only assert exit
139	  code + PC range. A wrong register value passes all of them.
140	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_*.sh`
141	- Status: not-started
142	
143	### F-T3.2 Untested trigger-mode combinations
144	
145	- Trigger: csr-entry + icount-exit; slice-entry + pc-exit;
146	  BBV-emit with simultaneous icount; etc. ~32 entry/exit pairings
147	  exist; suite tests CSR+CSR and PC+PC only.
148	- Breaks: Plugin or vsim bug in a cross-mode path is invisible until
149	  hit in production.
150	- Source: gap in `run_e2e.sh` mode coverage
151	- Status: not-started
152	
153	### F-T3.3 Submodule version drift
154	
155	- Trigger: Upstream QEMU 9.x changes TCG plugin ABI; vsim refactors
156	  the DM; SimPoint pinned to 17783e5 stays unmaintained.
157	- Breaks: Plugin silently produces wrong state, or fails to build,
158	  with no CI gate.
159	- Source: `.gitmodules`; no CI matrix against upstream snapshots
160	- Status: not-started
161	
162	### F-T3.4 vlenb mismatch silent
163	
164	- Trigger: Fixture built with `--march=rv64gcv` for VLEN=512 (vlenb=64)
165	  runs on a vsim configured for VLEN=256 (vlenb=32) - or vice versa.
166	- Breaks: No cross-check that `vlenb_qemu == vlenb_vsim`. ResumeDriver
167	  reads only `vlenb` bytes of V-reg payload; upper bytes left as
168	  uninitialized scratch. Garbage in V regs.
169	- Source: gap in `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` and ABI
170	  validator
171	- Status: not-started
172	
173	### F-T3.5 Failure-injection holes
174	
175	- Trigger: Partial state drain mid-handoff; SIGKILL; disk full; mmap
176	  truncated to wrong size; corrupt magic with valid struct size.
177	- Breaks: Existing negative drivers cover only 4 scenarios
178	  (corrupt-state-short-read, no-qemu-binary, gdbstub-timeout,
179	  pty-handback). Subtle resource-exhaustion or race-condition paths
180	  unguarded.
181	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_{corrupt_state,no_qemu_binary,gdbstub_timeout,pty_handback}.sh`
182	- Status: not-started
183	
184	### F-T3.6 Determinism across re-runs
185	
186	- Trigger: Run the same ELF twice; diff the drained state files.
187	- Breaks: No guarantee of byte-identical output. `reserved[]` bytes,
188	  CSR drain order, and FP rounding sources could vary. Any test using
189	  exact-equality assertion is flaky.
190	- Source: `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` drain order; ABI reserved
191	  bytes
192	- Status: not-started
193	
194	### F-T3.7 Self-modifying code
195	
196	- Trigger: JIT or workload writes to .text inside ROI.
197	- Breaks: Shared mmap is live so the write is visible immediately,
198	  but QEMU1 has cached the old insn in TCG and vsim's icache starts
199	  cold. Same binary -> different trace.
200	- Source: documented out-of-scope in `hybrid_plan.md` Section 2.5;
201	  not detected
202	- Status: not-started
203	
204	### F-T3.8 GDB handshake timeout / zombie QEMU2
205	
206	- Trigger: QEMU2 launches but its gdbstub does not respond within 5s,
207	  or responds then hangs mid-P-packet.
208	- Breaks: `qemu_handback::connect_loopback` may time out but the
209	  spawned QEMU2 child can be left as a zombie if the orchestrator
210	  has no SIGALRM watchdog.
211	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`
212	- Status: not-started
213	
214	## Highest-leverage next moves
215	
216	1. **Add register-readback assertions to demos T3-T7** - cheapest fix
217	   for F-T3.1. Single gdbstub `g` packet at QEMU2 entry, memcmp
218	   against the drained `state.bin`.
219	2. **Land `docs/v_csr_plan.md`** - already a 9-step TDD sequence;
220	   partially addresses F-T1.2 and F-T1.3 by establishing the
221	   ResumeDriver pattern for Tier-A and Tier-B CSRs.
222	3. **Promote `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5 hardening to a tracked ROADMAP** -
223	   covers F-T1.2, F-T1.3, F-T1.4, F-T1.5, F-T2.1, F-T2.3, F-T2.5 in
224	   one scoped effort. Currently never scheduled.

file.content:
# TODO: Failure Modes Backlog

Prioritized list of conditions under which the hybrid QEMU+vsim simulator
will silently fail or hang on real workloads. Derived from a 2026-05-17
audit of `qemu_plugin/`, `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`,
`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`, and the test suite at
`hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/`.

Format: one row per failure mode.

- Trigger: the production scenario that exposes the bug
- Breaks: the observable symptom
- Source: file(s) where the gap lives
- Status: not-started | in-progress | done

## Tier 1 - Will fail on the next real workload

### F-T1.1 Semihosting inside ROI

- Trigger: Any C fixture calling `printf`, `malloc`, `_exit`, or any
  libgloss-routed syscall between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()`.
- Breaks: libgloss_vh emits the semihosting magic triple
  (`slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`); vsim's trap handler
  `runtime/trap.S` falls into `handler.c` which calls `_exit(mcause)`,
  itself a semihosting ebreak. Infinite trap loop / test timeout.
- Source: `docs/vsim_semihosting_investigation.md` (unsolved);
  `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/{trap.S,handler.c}`
- Status: not-started

### F-T1.2 FP state not restored to vsim

- Trigger: Any workload using f0..f31 inside ROI.
- Breaks: QEMU1 plugin drains FPRs into `state.bin`. ResumeDriver does
  not write FPRs. vsim FPRs remain at RTL reset value. StateDrain
  passes through the stale QEMU1 snapshot to QEMU2 unchanged.
  FP computations in ROI silently corrupt register contents.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`,
  `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`
- Status: fix it

### F-T1.3 PMP state not restored to vsim

- Trigger: Any workload depending on PMP-enforced memory regions
  inside ROI.
- Breaks: Plugin drains pmpcfg[0..15] and pmpaddr[0..63] but
  ResumeDriver writes none. vsim sees zeroed PMP entries; any
  write-protect or execute-lock setup from QEMU1 evaporates.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`
- Status: not-started

### F-T1.4 Exception state lost across vsim ROI

- Trigger: Any trap (illegal-insn, page-fault, alignment, ebreak) fires
  inside ROI.
- Breaks: StateDrain returns the QEMU1 snapshot for mcause/mtval/mepc
  instead of vsim's current trap state. QEMU2 receives stale values;
  any post-handoff trap diagnostic logic is wrong.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` (passthrough path)
- Status: not-started

### F-T1.5 LR/SC reservation not transferred

- Trigger: An lr.{w,d} retires before the trigger and the matching
  sc.{w,d} retires after - or any atomic across the handoff.
- Breaks: Reservation is not in the ABI struct, never round-trips.
  sc.* always fails on re-entry, so any `__atomic_compare_exchange`
  loop spins forever.
- Source: `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (missing field); resume/drain
  both
- Status: not-started

## Tier 2 - Will fail when scaling

### F-T2.1 priv mode hardcoded to M

- Trigger: Linux/kernel workload running in S- or U-mode at the
  trigger boundary.
- Breaks: `state_drain.hpp:40` and `resume_driver.hpp:40` force
  `priv=3`; DCSR.prv never written. `dret` always resumes in M-mode.
  S/U code raises illegal-insn -> trap loop.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`,
  `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.2 Workload exceeding 128MB mmap

- Trigger: Heap, stack, or static data extending past the default 128MB
  shared mmap.
- Breaks: vsim's SimpleMemory bounds reject the access or silently
  truncate; QEMU sees MMIO instead of RAM. No startup-time bounds
  check on ELF segments against mmap size.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/platform/simple_memory.hpp`,
  orchestrator mmap sizing
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.3 Multi-hart resume path

- Trigger: `_NDS_NHART=2` configuration; workload using hart 1, atomic
  fences, IPIs, or hart-to-hart sync.
- Breaks: ABI carries `hart_id` and per-hart arrays but ResumeDriver
  and StateDrain only handle hart 0. Resuming hart 1 from hart 0's
  state corrupts both.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/{resume_driver,state_drain}.hpp`,
  `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (thread selection)
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.4 mcycle/minstret monotonicity

- Trigger: Long-running workload; or any consumer reading these CSRs
  across the handoff.
- Breaks: No test asserts monotonicity. If the icount trigger is off
  by one, vsim's mcycle could be less than QEMU1's exit mcycle and
  nothing would catch it.
- Source: gap in test suite; ABI does carry mcycle/minstret
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.5 MMIO inside ROI

- Trigger: Workload calls `putchar`, touches UART/PLIC, accesses ROM,
  or hits aliased memory inside ROI.
- Breaks: Phase-5 interconnect watch (`hybrid_plan.md` Section 5) was
  never implemented. Writes to MMIO ranges either vanish or hit
  SimpleMemory as if they were RAM. QEMU2 device state desyncs from
  vsim's view.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/platform/interconnect.hpp` (no watch);
  `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5
- Status: not-started

## Tier 3 - Bug surfaces invisible to current suite

### F-T3.1 State-value opacity in M3-M7 demos

- Trigger: Any bug that copies the wrong register, byte-swaps, or
  truncates a 64-bit value during M3 (icount), M4 (BBV), M5
  (slice-consume), M6 (archive), or M7 (replay).
- Breaks: Demos `demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`,
  `demo_bbv_profile.sh`, `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`,
  `demo_archive_replay.sh`, `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` only assert exit
  code + PC range. A wrong register value passes all of them.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_*.sh`
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.2 Untested trigger-mode combinations

- Trigger: csr-entry + icount-exit; slice-entry + pc-exit;
  BBV-emit with simultaneous icount; etc. ~32 entry/exit pairings
  exist; suite tests CSR+CSR and PC+PC only.
- Breaks: Plugin or vsim bug in a cross-mode path is invisible until
  hit in production.
- Source: gap in `run_e2e.sh` mode coverage
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.3 Submodule version drift

- Trigger: Upstream QEMU 9.x changes TCG plugin ABI; vsim refactors
  the DM; SimPoint pinned to 17783e5 stays unmaintained.
- Breaks: Plugin silently produces wrong state, or fails to build,
  with no CI gate.
- Source: `.gitmodules`; no CI matrix against upstream snapshots
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.4 vlenb mismatch silent

- Trigger: Fixture built with `--march=rv64gcv` for VLEN=512 (vlenb=64)
  runs on a vsim configured for VLEN=256 (vlenb=32) - or vice versa.
- Breaks: No cross-check that `vlenb_qemu == vlenb_vsim`. ResumeDriver
  reads only `vlenb` bytes of V-reg payload; upper bytes left as
  uninitialized scratch. Garbage in V regs.
- Source: gap in `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` and ABI
  validator
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.5 Failure-injection holes

- Trigger: Partial state drain mid-handoff; SIGKILL; disk full; mmap
  truncated to wrong size; corrupt magic with valid struct size.
- Breaks: Existing negative drivers cover only 4 scenarios
  (corrupt-state-short-read, no-qemu-binary, gdbstub-timeout,
  pty-handback). Subtle resource-exhaustion or race-condition paths
  unguarded.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_{corrupt_state,no_qemu_binary,gdbstub_timeout,pty_handback}.sh`
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.6 Determinism across re-runs

- Trigger: Run the same ELF twice; diff the drained state files.
- Breaks: No guarantee of byte-identical output. `reserved[]` bytes,
  CSR drain order, and FP rounding sources could vary. Any test using
  exact-equality assertion is flaky.
- Source: `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` drain order; ABI reserved
  bytes
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.7 Self-modifying code

- Trigger: JIT or workload writes to .text inside ROI.
- Breaks: Shared mmap is live so the write is visible immediately,
  but QEMU1 has cached the old insn in TCG and vsim's icache starts
  cold. Same binary -> different trace.
- Source: documented out-of-scope in `hybrid_plan.md` Section 2.5;
  not detected
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.8 GDB handshake timeout / zombie QEMU2

- Trigger: QEMU2 launches but its gdbstub does not respond within 5s,
  or responds then hangs mid-P-packet.
- Breaks: `qemu_handback::connect_loopback` may time out but the
  spawned QEMU2 child can be left as a zombie if the orchestrator
  has no SIGALRM watchdog.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`
- Status: not-started

## Highest-leverage next moves

1. **Add register-readback assertions to demos T3-T7** - cheapest fix
   for F-T3.1. Single gdbstub `g` packet at QEMU2 entry, memcmp
   against the drained `state.bin`.
2. **Land `docs/v_csr_plan.md`** - already a 9-step TDD sequence;
   partially addresses F-T1.2 and F-T1.3 by establishing the
   ResumeDriver pattern for Tier-A and Tier-B CSRs.
3. **Promote `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5 hardening to a tracked ROADMAP** -
   covers F-T1.2, F-T1.3, F-T1.4, F-T1.5, F-T2.1, F-T2.3, F-T2.5 in
   one scoped effort. Currently never scheduled.

109	target_include_directories(test_v_regfile_xfer PRIVATE
110	  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
111	)
112	add_dependencies(check test_v_regfile_xfer)

114	if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
115	  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
116	    message(WARNING
117	      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
118	      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
119	      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
120	  endif()
121	  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")
122	
123	  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
124	  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
125	  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
126	  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
127	  # from the host's build/.
128	  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
129	    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
130	    "rt_pc_jump:0"
131	    "rt_sign_bits:0"
132	    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
133	    "rt_long_roi:0"
134	    "rt_all_gprs:0"
135	    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
136	    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
137	    "rt_csr_survival:0"
138	  )
139	  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
140	    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
141	    list(GET parts 0 fname)
142	    list(GET parts 1 erc)
143	    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
144	    add_test(
145	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
146	      COMMAND bash -c
147	        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
148	    )
149	    # COST: ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the
150	    # tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
151	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
152	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
153	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
154	      COST 30
155	    )
156	    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
157	    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
158	    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
159	    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
160	    add_test(
161	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
162	      COMMAND bash -c
163	        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
164	    )
165	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
166	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
167	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
168	      COST 30
169	    )
170	  endforeach()
171	
172	  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
173	  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
174	  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
175	  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
176	  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
177	  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
178	  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
179	    "rt_shared_mem:0"
180	    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
181	    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
182	    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
183	    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
184	    "rt_c_hello:0"
185	    "rt_c_printf:0"
186	    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
187	    # Regression for the "no redundant ELF reload" rule: .bss survival
188	    # across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 without users hand-allocating fixed
189	    # addresses. Fails today because vsim's elf_loader and QEMU 2's
190	    # -kernel both zero-fill the .bss tail of every PT_LOAD.
191	    "rt_c_static_buf:0"
192	    # 1D 3-tap FIR convolution as a real RVV use case spanning the
193	    # handoff: QEMU1 generates x via intrinsics; vsim applies the kernel
194	    # (vslide1up / vslide1down + vadd) using x carried in its compiler-
195	    # picked V register; QEMU2 stores y and scalar-verifies. V regfile
196	    # transport rides on the save-all / reload-all helpers in
197	    # runtime/v_regfile_xfer.{h,c} so the fixture body is pure intrinsics
198	    # with no physical V register references.
199	    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
200	    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
201	    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
202	    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
203	    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
204	    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
205	    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
206	    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
207	  )
208	  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
209	    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
210	    list(GET parts 0 fname)
211	    list(GET parts 1 erc)
212	    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
213	    set(timeout_env "")
214	    set(tcost 60)
215	    if(nparts GREATER 2)
216	      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
217	      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
218	      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
219	      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
220	      set(tcost ${tsec})
221	    endif()
222	    # CSR variant.
223	    add_test(
224	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
225	      COMMAND bash -c
226	        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
227	    )
228	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
229	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
230	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
231	      COST ${tcost}
232	    )
233	    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
234	    add_test(
235	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
236	      COMMAND bash -c
237	        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
238	    )
239	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
240	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
241	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
242	      COST ${tcost}
243	    )
244	  endforeach()
245	
246	  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
247	  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
248	  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
249	  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
250	  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
251	  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
252	    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
253	      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
254	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
255	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
256	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
257	    )
258	  endforeach()
259	
260	  # Contract test: QEMU plugin exits 200 on drain (matches vsim's
261	  # kExitHandoffToQemu). Guards the F1 rebrand from regressing back to
262	  # exit(0), which would make workload-completion indistinguishable from
263	  # a drained handoff under the orchestrator.
264	  add_test(NAME test_plugin_drain_exit_code
265	    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh)
266	  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_drain_exit_code PROPERTIES
267	    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
268	    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
269	  )
270	
271	  # F5: plugin icount-trigger mode. Runs a CSR-less spin fixture with
272	  # icount=N; asserts drain at exit 200 and minstret >= N.
273	  add_test(NAME test_plugin_icount_trigger
274	    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh)
275	  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_icount_trigger PROPERTIES
276	    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
277	    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
278	  )
279	
280	  # F7: plugin BBV-emit mode (SimPoint profile). Runs the CSR-less spin
281	  # with bbv_out= + slice= + icount=; asserts the .bb file has the
282	  # expected row count and is consumable by tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint.
283	  add_test(NAME test_plugin_bbv_emit
284	    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh)
285	  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_bbv_emit PROPERTIES
286	    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
287	    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
288	  )
289	
290	  # F11: plugin BBV slice-consume mode. Runs the CSR-less spin with
291	  # slice= + slice_at=K + icount=fallback; asserts drain at the end of
292	  # slice index K (0-based), before the icount fallback fires.
293	  add_test(NAME test_plugin_phase_consume
294	    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_phase_consume.sh)
295	  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_phase_consume PROPERTIES
296	    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
297	    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
298	  )
299	
300	  # Switching-mode demo suite (T1-T7): one labeled demo per M1-M8 mode,
301	  # plus the centerpiece SimPoint pipeline. Each demo wraps an existing
302	  # harness or chains plugin + simpoint + orchestrator into a single
303	  # user-facing flow. Labeled "switching-mode" so contributors can run
304	  # the subset with `ctest -L switching-mode`. T7 (full simpoint loop)
305	  # has a higher cost: profile pass + 2 phase drains + 2 vsim replays.
306	  set(VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS
307	    "demo_csr_oneshot:30"
308	    "demo_pc_oneshot:30"
309	    "demo_icount_roundtrip:60"
310	    "demo_bbv_profile:30"
311	    "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip:60"
312	    "demo_archive_replay:60"
313	    "demo_simpoint_loop:180"
314	  )
315	  foreach(entry ${VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS})
316	    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
317	    list(GET parts 0 dname)
318	    list(GET parts 1 dcost)
319	    add_test(NAME test_${dname}
320	      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/${dname}.sh)
321	    set_tests_properties(test_${dname} PROPERTIES
322	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
323	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;switching-mode"
324	      COST ${dcost}
325	    )
326	  endforeach()
327	endif()

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_site tests/hybrid/handoff_site.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_site PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_site)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_from_qemu)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_drain PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_drain)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_qemu_handback tests/hybrid/qemu_handback.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_qemu_handback PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_qemu_handback)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

vsim_add_test(test_icount_trigger tests/hybrid/icount_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_icount_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_icount_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_v_regfile_xfer tests/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_v_regfile_xfer PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_v_regfile_xfer)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    # COST: ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the
    # tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST 30
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST 30
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # Regression for the "no redundant ELF reload" rule: .bss survival
    # across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 without users hand-allocating fixed
    # addresses. Fails today because vsim's elf_loader and QEMU 2's
    # -kernel both zero-fill the .bss tail of every PT_LOAD.
    "rt_c_static_buf:0"
    # 1D 3-tap FIR convolution as a real RVV use case spanning the
    # handoff: QEMU1 generates x via intrinsics; vsim applies the kernel
    # (vslide1up / vslide1down + vadd) using x carried in its compiler-
    # picked V register; QEMU2 stores y and scalar-verifies. V regfile
    # transport rides on the save-all / reload-all helpers in
    # runtime/v_regfile_xfer.{h,c} so the fixture body is pure intrinsics
    # with no physical V register references.
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Contract test: QEMU plugin exits 200 on drain (matches vsim's
  # kExitHandoffToQemu). Guards the F1 rebrand from regressing back to
  # exit(0), which would make workload-completion indistinguishable from
  # a drained handoff under the orchestrator.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_drain_exit_code
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_drain_exit_code PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F5: plugin icount-trigger mode. Runs a CSR-less spin fixture with
  # icount=N; asserts drain at exit 200 and minstret >= N.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_icount_trigger
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_icount_trigger PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F7: plugin BBV-emit mode (SimPoint profile). Runs the CSR-less spin
  # with bbv_out= + slice= + icount=; asserts the .bb file has the
  # expected row count and is consumable by tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_bbv_emit
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_bbv_emit PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F11: plugin BBV slice-consume mode. Runs the CSR-less spin with
  # slice= + slice_at=K + icount=fallback; asserts drain at the end of
  # slice index K (0-based), before the icount fallback fires.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_phase_consume
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_phase_consume.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_phase_consume PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # Switching-mode demo suite (T1-T7): one labeled demo per M1-M8 mode,
  # plus the centerpiece SimPoint pipeline. Each demo wraps an existing
  # harness or chains plugin + simpoint + orchestrator into a single
  # user-facing flow. Labeled "switching-mode" so contributors can run
  # the subset with `ctest -L switching-mode`. T7 (full simpoint loop)
  # has a higher cost: profile pass + 2 phase drains + 2 vsim replays.
  set(VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS
    "demo_csr_oneshot:30"
    "demo_pc_oneshot:30"
    "demo_icount_roundtrip:60"
    "demo_bbv_profile:30"
    "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip:60"
    "demo_archive_replay:60"
    "demo_simpoint_loop:180"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 dname)
    list(GET parts 1 dcost)
    add_test(NAME test_${dname}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/${dname}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_${dname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;switching-mode"
      COST ${dcost}
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# TODO: Failure Modes Backlog

Prioritized list of conditions under which the hybrid QEMU+vsim simulator
will silently fail or hang on real workloads. Derived from a 2026-05-17
audit of `qemu_plugin/`, `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`,
`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`, and the test suite at
`hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/`.

Format: one row per failure mode.

- Trigger: the production scenario that exposes the bug
- Breaks: the observable symptom
- Source: file(s) where the gap lives
- Status: not-started | in-progress | done

## Tier 1 - Will fail on the next real workload

### F-T1.1 Semihosting inside ROI

- Trigger: Any C fixture calling `printf`, `malloc`, `_exit`, or any
  libgloss-routed syscall between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()`.
- Breaks: libgloss_vh emits the semihosting magic triple
  (`slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`); vsim's trap handler
  `runtime/trap.S` falls into `handler.c` which calls `_exit(mcause)`,
  itself a semihosting ebreak. Infinite trap loop / test timeout.
- Source: `docs/vsim_semihosting_investigation.md` (unsolved);
  `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/{trap.S,handler.c}`
- Status: not-started

### F-T1.2 FP state not restored to vsim

- Trigger: Any workload using f0..f31 inside ROI.
- Breaks: QEMU1 plugin drains FPRs into `state.bin`. ResumeDriver does
  not write FPRs. vsim FPRs remain at RTL reset value. StateDrain
  passes through the stale QEMU1 snapshot to QEMU2 unchanged.
  FP computations in ROI silently corrupt register contents.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`,
  `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`
- Status: fix it

### F-T1.3 PMP state not restored to vsim

- Trigger: Any workload depending on PMP-enforced memory regions
  inside ROI.
- Breaks: Plugin drains pmpcfg[0..15] and pmpaddr[0..63] but
  ResumeDriver writes none. vsim sees zeroed PMP entries; any
  write-protect or execute-lock setup from QEMU1 evaporates.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`
- Status: not-started

### F-T1.4 Exception state lost across vsim ROI

- Trigger: Any trap (illegal-insn, page-fault, alignment, ebreak) fires
  inside ROI.
- Breaks: StateDrain returns the QEMU1 snapshot for mcause/mtval/mepc
  instead of vsim's current trap state. QEMU2 receives stale values;
  any post-handoff trap diagnostic logic is wrong.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` (passthrough path)
- Status: not-started

### F-T1.5 LR/SC reservation not transferred

- Trigger: An lr.{w,d} retires before the trigger and the matching
  sc.{w,d} retires after - or any atomic across the handoff.
- Breaks: Reservation is not in the ABI struct, never round-trips.
  sc.* always fails on re-entry, so any `__atomic_compare_exchange`
  loop spins forever.
- Source: `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (missing field); resume/drain
  both
- Status: not-started

## Tier 2 - Will fail when scaling

### F-T2.1 priv mode hardcoded to M

- Trigger: Linux/kernel workload running in S- or U-mode at the
  trigger boundary.
- Breaks: `state_drain.hpp:40` and `resume_driver.hpp:40` force
  `priv=3`; DCSR.prv never written. `dret` always resumes in M-mode.
  S/U code raises illegal-insn -> trap loop.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`,
  `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.2 Workload exceeding 128MB mmap

- Trigger: Heap, stack, or static data extending past the default 128MB
  shared mmap.
- Breaks: vsim's SimpleMemory bounds reject the access or silently
  truncate; QEMU sees MMIO instead of RAM. No startup-time bounds
  check on ELF segments against mmap size.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/platform/simple_memory.hpp`,
  orchestrator mmap sizing
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.3 Multi-hart resume path

- Trigger: `_NDS_NHART=2` configuration; workload using hart 1, atomic
  fences, IPIs, or hart-to-hart sync.
- Breaks: ABI carries `hart_id` and per-hart arrays but ResumeDriver
  and StateDrain only handle hart 0. Resuming hart 1 from hart 0's
  state corrupts both.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/{resume_driver,state_drain}.hpp`,
  `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (thread selection)
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.4 mcycle/minstret monotonicity

- Trigger: Long-running workload; or any consumer reading these CSRs
  across the handoff.
- Breaks: No test asserts monotonicity. If the icount trigger is off
  by one, vsim's mcycle could be less than QEMU1's exit mcycle and
  nothing would catch it.
- Source: gap in test suite; ABI does carry mcycle/minstret
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.5 MMIO inside ROI

- Trigger: Workload calls `putchar`, touches UART/PLIC, accesses ROM,
  or hits aliased memory inside ROI.
- Breaks: Phase-5 interconnect watch (`hybrid_plan.md` Section 5) was
  never implemented. Writes to MMIO ranges either vanish or hit
  SimpleMemory as if they were RAM. QEMU2 device state desyncs from
  vsim's view.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/platform/interconnect.hpp` (no watch);
  `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5
- Status: not-started

## Tier 3 - Bug surfaces invisible to current suite

### F-T3.1 State-value opacity in M3-M7 demos

- Trigger: Any bug that copies the wrong register, byte-swaps, or
  truncates a 64-bit value during M3 (icount), M4 (BBV), M5
  (slice-consume), M6 (archive), or M7 (replay).
- Breaks: Demos `demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`,
  `demo_bbv_profile.sh`, `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`,
  `demo_archive_replay.sh`, `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` only assert exit
  code + PC range. A wrong register value passes all of them.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_*.sh`
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.2 Untested trigger-mode combinations

- Trigger: csr-entry + icount-exit; slice-entry + pc-exit;
  BBV-emit with simultaneous icount; etc. ~32 entry/exit pairings
  exist; suite tests CSR+CSR and PC+PC only.
- Breaks: Plugin or vsim bug in a cross-mode path is invisible until
  hit in production.
- Source: gap in `run_e2e.sh` mode coverage
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.3 Submodule version drift

- Trigger: Upstream QEMU 9.x changes TCG plugin ABI; vsim refactors
  the DM; SimPoint pinned to 17783e5 stays unmaintained.
- Breaks: Plugin silently produces wrong state, or fails to build,
  with no CI gate.
- Source: `.gitmodules`; no CI matrix against upstream snapshots
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.4 vlenb mismatch silent

- Trigger: Fixture built with `--march=rv64gcv` for VLEN=512 (vlenb=64)
  runs on a vsim configured for VLEN=256 (vlenb=32) - or vice versa.
- Breaks: No cross-check that `vlenb_qemu == vlenb_vsim`. ResumeDriver
  reads only `vlenb` bytes of V-reg payload; upper bytes left as
  uninitialized scratch. Garbage in V regs.
- Source: gap in `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` and ABI
  validator
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.5 Failure-injection holes

- Trigger: Partial state drain mid-handoff; SIGKILL; disk full; mmap
  truncated to wrong size; corrupt magic with valid struct size.
- Breaks: Existing negative drivers cover only 4 scenarios
  (corrupt-state-short-read, no-qemu-binary, gdbstub-timeout,
  pty-handback). Subtle resource-exhaustion or race-condition paths
  unguarded.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_{corrupt_state,no_qemu_binary,gdbstub_timeout,pty_handback}.sh`
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.6 Determinism across re-runs

- Trigger: Run the same ELF twice; diff the drained state files.
- Breaks: No guarantee of byte-identical output. `reserved[]` bytes,
  CSR drain order, and FP rounding sources could vary. Any test using
  exact-equality assertion is flaky.
- Source: `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` drain order; ABI reserved
  bytes
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.7 Self-modifying code

- Trigger: JIT or workload writes to .text inside ROI.
- Breaks: Shared mmap is live so the write is visible immediately,
  but QEMU1 has cached the old insn in TCG and vsim's icache starts
  cold. Same binary -> different trace.
- Source: documented out-of-scope in `hybrid_plan.md` Section 2.5;
  not detected
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.8 GDB handshake timeout / zombie QEMU2

- Trigger: QEMU2 launches but its gdbstub does not respond within 5s,
  or responds then hangs mid-P-packet.
- Breaks: `qemu_handback::connect_loopback` may time out but the
  spawned QEMU2 child can be left as a zombie if the orchestrator
  has no SIGALRM watchdog.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`
- Status: not-started

## Highest-leverage next moves

1. **Add register-readback assertions to demos T3-T7** - cheapest fix
   for F-T3.1. Single gdbstub `g` packet at QEMU2 entry, memcmp
   against the drained `state.bin`.
2. **Land `docs/v_csr_plan.md`** - already a 9-step TDD sequence;
   partially addresses F-T1.2 and F-T1.3 by establishing the
   ResumeDriver pattern for Tier-A and Tier-B CSRs.
3. **Promote `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5 hardening to a tracked ROADMAP** -
   covers F-T1.2, F-T1.3, F-T1.4, F-T1.5, F-T2.1, F-T2.3, F-T2.5 in
   one scoped effort. Currently never scheduled.

git -C hybrid_vsim add src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp && \
git -C hybrid_vsim commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
behavioral: F-T1.2 restore FP state across hybrid handoff

ResumeDriver now writes f0..f31 to the vsim hart via DM Abstract
Commands when HYBRID_FLAG_FP is set, gated on mstatus.FS being
enabled (FS=Off makes the hart NACK the FPR writes). StateDrain's
drain_with_passthrough mirrors the V-CSR pattern: it freshly reads
f0..f31 from the DM when the FP flag is set, instead of leaking the
stale QEMU 1 snapshot through to QEMU 2.

Closes failure mode F-T1.2 (TODO.md): FP computations executed
inside the vsim ROI were previously silently dropped because the
restore path was missing entirely and the drain path was a
pass-through of the pre-handoff register file.

Test coverage:
- resume programs f0..f31 via Abstract Commands when FLAG_FP set
- mstatus write precedes FPR writes (FS enable ordering)
- FPR DATA0/DATA1 staging carries state.fpr[i] verbatim
- no FPR Abstract Commands issued when FLAG_FP unset
- drain_with_passthrough reads FPRs from DM when FLAG_FP set
- FP reads sequenced after GPR reads
- pass-through fallback retained when FLAG_FP unset

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - User invoked `/loop 5m read TODO.md and implement the feature with "Status: fix it"` — a recurring 5-minute cron-triggered task to find TODO.md entries flagged with `Status: fix it` and implement them.
   - User had been repeatedly issuing variants of "continue the next phase in TEST_ROADMAP.md" (already fully done) and asking for failure-mode implementation work. The `/loop` formalizes this into an automated workflow.
   - The session bridged from a compacted prior conversation that had produced TODO.md (18 failure modes from a 2026-05-17 audit) and confirmed TEST_ROADMAP.md was fully complete.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Hybrid QEMU+vsim co-simulator three-phase handoff (QEMU1 → vsim → QEMU2)
   - `hybrid_state_v1` wire ABI struct (GPRs, FPRs, CSRs, PMP, V-state)
   - RISC-V Debug Module Abstract Commands for register access via DMI
   - Trigger modes: CSR-insn-match, PC-symbol-resolve
   - mstatus.FS (bits 13:14, mask 0x6000) for FP enable; mstatus.VS (bits 9:10, mask 0x600) for V enable
   - Register number encoding: `regno_gpr(i) = 0x1000+i`, `regno_fpr(i) = 0x1020+i`, `regno_csr(csr) = csr`
   - doctest single-header unit testing framework
   - TDD discipline: RED → GREEN → REFACTOR with structural/behavioral commit split
   - Header-only hybrid layer (src/hybrid/*.hpp) buildable with g++ + doctest standalone
   - DM Abstract Commands' cmderr semantics (sticky, W1C-cleared)
   - Program Buffer (PB) fallback pattern with scratch GPR x5

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/TODO.md`
     - Source of work: contained F-T1.2 entry with `Status: fix it` (line 39)
     - Modified: F-T1.2 status flipped from "fix it" → "done"

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`
     - Core change: ResumeDriver writes f0..f31 to vsim via DM Abstract Commands when HYBRID_FLAG_FP is set
     - Restructured mstatus write to be unified gate for both FP and V extensions
     - Key new code (inserted after halt poll, before V block):
     ```cpp
     const bool fp_needs_fs = (state.flags & HYBRID_FLAG_FP)
                           && (state.mstatus & 0x6000u);
     const bool v_needs_vs  = (state.flags & HYBRID_FLAG_V)
                           && (state.mstatus & 0x600u);
     if (fp_needs_fs || v_needs_vs)
       write_csr_abstract_or_pb(kCsrMstatus, state.mstatus);

     if (state.flags & HYBRID_FLAG_FP) {
       for (uint16_t i = 0; i < 32; ++i)
         write_64bit_register(regno_fpr(i), state.fpr[i]);
     }
     ```
     - Removed: the original `if (state.mstatus & 0x600u) write_csr_abstract_or_pb(kCsrMstatus, ...)` inside the V block

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`
     - Core change: drain_with_passthrough freshly reads f0..f31 when HYBRID_FLAG_FP is set (was pass-through from input)
     - Inserted between GPR/PC copy and V CSR block:
     ```cpp
     if (input.flags & HYBRID_FLAG_FP) {
       for (uint16_t i = 0; i < 32; ++i)
         state.fpr[i] = read_64bit_register(regno_fpr(i));
     }
     ```
     - Updated docstring to mention FP behavior

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp`
     - Renamed "resume does NOT program FP/CSR/PMP" → "resume does NOT program M-mode CSRs/PMP"; dropped FP setup and FPR negative assertion
     - Added 4 new test cases:
       a. "resume with HYBRID_FLAG_FP programs f0..f31 via Abstract Commands" - checks contains_regno(ns, regno_fpr(i)) for all 32 FPRs
       b. "resume with HYBRID_FLAG_FP: mstatus written before FPR Abstract Commands" - ordering via index comparison
       c. "resume with HYBRID_FLAG_FP: FPR writes carry the state value via DATA0/DATA1" - checks DATA0/DATA1 staging values match state.fpr[7]
       d. "resume without HYBRID_FLAG_FP: no FPR Abstract Commands issued"
     - Final: 18 cases, 210 assertions, all passing

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp`
     - Renamed "drain_with_passthrough copies FP/CSR/V slots" → "drain_with_passthrough copies CSR/PMP/V-payload slots"; removed HYBRID_FLAG_FP from flags and dropped FPR pass-through check
     - Added 3 new test cases:
       a. "drain_with_passthrough with HYBRID_FLAG_FP: FPRs read from DM, not input"
       b. "drain_with_passthrough with HYBRID_FLAG_FP: FP reads issued after GPR reads"
       c. "drain_with_passthrough without HYBRID_FLAG_FP: no FPR Abstract Commands issued"
     - Used `bool is_fpr = ...; CHECK_FALSE(is_fpr);` pattern to avoid doctest's `&&` restriction
     - Final: 13 cases, 450 assertions, all passing

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/recording_bus.hpp`
     - Read for reference - shared DmiBus mock with `regfile` map auto-latches `pending_data0/1` on read commands

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/abstract_command.hpp`
     - Read - confirms `regno_fpr(i)` helper already exists

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/include/hybrid/state_abi.h`
     - Read - confirms `uint64_t fpr[32]` field in hybrid_state_v1 struct

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` (grep only)
     - Line 248: `s.fpr[i] = read_reg64(v->fpr[i]);` - plugin already drains FPRs
     - Line 130: registers FPR by name

   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (grep only)
     - Line 302: `try_write_named_u64(sess, reg_map, hybrid_fpr_names[i], state.fpr[i])` - already sends FPRs to QEMU2 via gdbstub P-packets

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Shell cd persistence**: `cd hybrid_vsim && git diff` left the shell inside hybrid_vsim/; subsequent `git -C hybrid_vsim` failed because the relative path no longer resolves. Fixed by using absolute path `cd /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim`.
   - **Submodule path rejection**: `git add hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/...` from top repo failed with "is in submodule". Fixed by adding files from within the submodule directory.
   - **doctest CHECK_FALSE with `&&`**: Compile error "Expression Too Complex Please Rewrite As Binary Comparison". Fixed by extracting to a local bool: `const bool is_fpr = regno >= regno_fpr(0) && regno <= regno_fpr(31); CHECK_FALSE(is_fpr);`

5. Problem Solving:
   - **F-T1.2 root cause closed**: The hybrid handoff pipeline had a complete drain side (QEMU plugin → state.bin) and complete restore-to-QEMU2 side (gdbstub P-packets), but the middle stage (state.bin → vsim → updated state.bin) was missing both the resume write and the drain read for FPRs. The vsim was running with reset-value FPRs (zeros) and propagating those back. Fix establishes:
     - mstatus.FS gated write so the hart accepts FPR abstract commands
     - 32 abstract command writes for f0..f31 in ResumeDriver
     - 32 abstract command reads for f0..f31 in StateDrain.drain_with_passthrough
   - **Cache-window scheduling**: Picked 270s ScheduleWakeup to stay inside 5-min prompt cache TTL.

6. All user messages:
   - Initial /loop command: "/loop 5m read TODO.md and implement the feature with 'Status: fix it'"
   - Second iteration (cron-triggered): "continue the next phase in TEST_ROADMAP.md if all dependence are ready\nread TODO.md and implement the feature with 'Status: fix it'"
   - (The session started with a /compact summary from a prior conversation)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - None. F-T1.2 is the only TODO.md entry that had "Status: fix it"; it is now done. The cron continues firing every 5 minutes but will be a no-op unless the user adds new `Status: fix it` markers to TODO.md.
   - Untouched failure modes still in TODO.md (all "Status: not-started"): F-T1.1, F-T1.3, F-T1.4, F-T1.5 (Tier 1); F-T2.1..F-T2.5 (Tier 2); F-T3.1..F-T3.8 (Tier 3). These would only be acted on if the user flips one's status to "fix it".

   The most recent substantive work was the second iteration of the cron-fired /loop:
   - Grepped TODO.md for "Status: fix it" → "No matches found"
   - Reported to user: "No entries marked `Status: fix it` in TODO.md. F-T1.2 was the only one, completed in commit `59be1af`. TEST_ROADMAP.md remains fully `done`. Nothing to do this iteration."

   - Top repo commit `1ef2fbd`: "behavioral: track TODO.md failure backlog; mark F-T1.2 done"
   
   Followed by ScheduleWakeup at 270s to align with the cron cycle while keeping the prompt cache warm.

9. Optional Next Step:

The most recent user message via cron yielded zero matches, so the correct behavior was to report nothing-to-do and wait.
