156	  immediately on connect; assert strictly increasing. Wire into
157	  `run_e2e.sh` so every Tier-2 fixture exercises it.
158	- Status: not-started
159	
160	### F-T2.5 MMIO inside ROI
161	
162	- Trigger: Workload calls `putchar`, touches UART/PLIC, accesses ROM,
163	  or hits aliased memory inside ROI.
164	- Breaks: Phase-5 interconnect watch (`hybrid_plan.md` Section 5) was
165	  never implemented. Writes to MMIO ranges either vanish or hit
166	  SimpleMemory as if they were RAM. QEMU2 device state desyncs from
167	  vsim's view.
168	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/platform/interconnect.hpp` (no watch);
169	  `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5
170	- Fix: Define a single allowlist of RAM + permitted-MMIO ranges in
171	  `src/platform/interconnect.hpp`. SimpleMemory::read/write checks
172	  every access against the allowlist; out-of-range aborts with a
173	  diagnostic logging the offending PC + address. Implements the
174	  Phase-5 interconnect watch.
175	- Status: not-started
176	
177	## Tier 3 - Bug surfaces invisible to current suite
178	
179	### F-T3.1 State-value opacity in M3-M7 demos
180	
181	- Trigger: Any bug that copies the wrong register, byte-swaps, or
182	  truncates a 64-bit value during M3 (icount), M4 (BBV), M5
183	  (slice-consume), M6 (archive), or M7 (replay).
184	- Breaks: Demos `demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`,
185	  `demo_bbv_profile.sh`, `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`,
186	  `demo_archive_replay.sh`, `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` only assert exit
187	  code + PC range. A wrong register value passes all of them.
188	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_*.sh`
189	- Fix: In each demo, after QEMU2 connects via gdbstub, issue a single
190	  `g` packet to read the full register file; parse and memcmp byte
191	  by byte against the corresponding fields in state.bin. Fail the
192	  demo on mismatch. ~30 lines of bash per demo via a shared helper.
193	- Status: not-started
194	
195	### F-T3.2 Untested trigger-mode combinations
196	
197	- Trigger: csr-entry + icount-exit; slice-entry + pc-exit;
198	  BBV-emit with simultaneous icount; etc. ~32 entry/exit pairings
199	  exist; suite tests CSR+CSR and PC+PC only.
200	- Breaks: Plugin or vsim bug in a cross-mode path is invisible until
201	  hit in production.
202	- Source: gap in `run_e2e.sh` mode coverage
203	- Fix: Parameterize `run_e2e.sh` with separate `ENTRY_MODE` and
204	  `EXIT_MODE` env vars (default = MODE if unset). Add a top-level
205	  `matrix` mode that iterates the cross-product across one canonical
206	  fixture, skipping pairings the plugin rejects up front.
207	- Status: not-started
208	
209	### F-T3.3 Submodule version drift
210	
211	- Trigger: Upstream QEMU 9.x changes TCG plugin ABI; vsim refactors
212	  the DM; SimPoint pinned to 17783e5 stays unmaintained.
213	- Breaks: Plugin silently produces wrong state, or fails to build,
214	  with no CI gate.
215	- Source: `.gitmodules`; no CI matrix against upstream snapshots
216	- Fix: Add `scripts/check_upstream_drift.sh` that, for each submodule,
217	  `git fetch`es the upstream remote and reports commits since the
218	  pinned SHA. Schedule a weekly CI job that runs the script and
219	  attaches the report to an auto-filed GitHub issue when non-empty.
220	- Status: not-started
221	
222	### F-T3.4 vlenb mismatch silent
223	
224	- Trigger: Fixture built with `--march=rv64gcv` for VLEN=512 (vlenb=64)
225	  runs on a vsim configured for VLEN=256 (vlenb=32) - or vice versa.
226	- Breaks: No cross-check that `vlenb_qemu == vlenb_vsim`. ResumeDriver
227	  reads only `vlenb` bytes of V-reg payload; upper bytes left as
228	  uninitialized scratch. Garbage in V regs.
229	- Source: gap in `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` and ABI
230	  validator
231	- Fix: At handoff entry, before any V transport, ResumeDriver reads
232	  the hart's `vlenb` CSR (0xC22) via Abstract Command and compares
233	  against `state.vlenb`. Mismatch aborts with a clear error naming
234	  both values. Same check on qemu_handback ingress side.
235	- Status: not-started
236	
237	### F-T3.5 Failure-injection holes
238	
239	- Trigger: Partial state drain mid-handoff; SIGKILL; disk full; mmap
240	  truncated to wrong size; corrupt magic with valid struct size.
241	- Breaks: Existing negative drivers cover only 4 scenarios
242	  (corrupt-state-short-read, no-qemu-binary, gdbstub-timeout,
243	  pty-handback). Subtle resource-exhaustion or race-condition paths
244	  unguarded.
245	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_{corrupt_state,no_qemu_binary,gdbstub_timeout,pty_handback}.sh`
246	- Fix: Add three negative drivers: `rt_fault_partial_drain.sh`
247	  (truncate state.bin to half its size mid-handoff),
248	  `rt_fault_disk_full.sh` (run on a size-capped tmpfs smaller than
249	  state size), `rt_fault_mmap_resize.sh` (truncate the shared mmap
250	  while vsim is mid-execution). Each asserts a clean diagnostic exit.
251	- Status: not-started
252	
253	### F-T3.6 Determinism across re-runs
254	
255	- Trigger: Run the same ELF twice; diff the drained state files.
256	- Breaks: No guarantee of byte-identical output. `reserved[]` bytes,
257	  CSR drain order, and FP rounding sources could vary. Any test using
258	  exact-equality assertion is flaky.
259	- Source: `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` drain order; ABI reserved
260	  bytes
261	- Fix: Add an e2e test that runs the same fixture twice and asserts
262	  `sha256sum state1.bin == sha256sum state2.bin`. Audit drain path:
263	  zero-initialise `reserved[]` explicitly in the plugin and in
264	  StateDrain; serialise CSR writes in canonical CSR-number order.
265	- Status: not-started
266	
267	### F-T3.7 Self-modifying code
268	
269	- Trigger: JIT or workload writes to .text inside ROI.
270	- Breaks: Shared mmap is live so the write is visible immediately,
271	  but QEMU1 has cached the old insn in TCG and vsim's icache starts
272	  cold. Same binary -> different trace.
273	- Source: documented out-of-scope in `hybrid_plan.md` Section 2.5;
274	  not detected
275	- Fix: At orchestrator startup, parse the ELF for `.text` PHDRs and
276	  call `mprotect(..., PROT_READ)` on the corresponding mmap pages.
277	  Any write fault becomes a clean SIGSEGV with a diagnostic naming
278	  the offending PC. SMC stays out-of-scope but is now detected
279	  instead of silently corrupting traces.
280	- Status: not-started
281	
282	### F-T3.8 GDB handshake timeout / zombie QEMU2
283	
284	- Trigger: QEMU2 launches but its gdbstub does not respond within 5s,
285	  or responds then hangs mid-P-packet.
286	- Breaks: `qemu_handback::connect_loopback` may time out but the
287	  spawned QEMU2 child can be left as a zombie if the orchestrator
288	  has no SIGALRM watchdog.
289	- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`
290	- Fix: In `connect_loopback`, install a `setitimer`-based watchdog
291	  before the gdb handshake. On SIGALRM, `kill(child_pid, SIGKILL)`
292	  then `waitpid(child_pid, ..., 0)` to reap, then throw. RAII guard
293	  ensures reap happens on any throw path.
294	- Status: not-started
295	
296	## Highest-leverage next moves
297	
298	1. **Add register-readback assertions to demos T3-T7** - cheapest fix
299	   for F-T3.1. Single gdbstub `g` packet at QEMU2 entry, memcmp
300	   against the drained `state.bin`.
301	2. **Land `docs/v_csr_plan.md`** - already a 9-step TDD sequence;
302	   partially addresses F-T1.2 and F-T1.3 by establishing the
303	   ResumeDriver pattern for Tier-A and Tier-B CSRs.
304	3. **Promote `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5 hardening to a tracked ROADMAP** -
305	   covers F-T1.2, F-T1.3, F-T1.4, F-T1.5, F-T2.1, F-T2.3, F-T2.5 in
306	   one scoped effort. Currently never scheduled.
307

file.content:
# TODO: Failure Modes Backlog

Prioritized list of conditions under which the hybrid QEMU+vsim simulator
will silently fail or hang on real workloads. Derived from a 2026-05-17
audit of `qemu_plugin/`, `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`,
`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`, and the test suite at
`hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/`.

Format: one row per failure mode.

- Trigger: the production scenario that exposes the bug
- Breaks: the observable symptom
- Source: file(s) where the gap lives
- Fix: the most feasible proposed solution
- Status: not-started | in-progress | done

## Tier 1 - Will fail on the next real workload

### F-T1.1 Semihosting inside ROI

- Trigger: Any C fixture calling `printf`, `malloc`, `_exit`, or any
  libgloss-routed syscall between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()`.
- Breaks: libgloss_vh emits the semihosting magic triple
  (`slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`); vsim's trap handler
  `runtime/trap.S` falls into `handler.c` which calls `_exit(mcause)`,
  itself a semihosting ebreak. Infinite trap loop / test timeout.
- Source: `docs/vsim_semihosting_investigation.md` (unsolved);
  `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/{trap.S,handler.c}`
- Fix: Drop libgloss_vh inside ROI. Compile a custom crt0 + handler.c
  that provides a local `_exit` (writes status word to an MMIO debug
  console region, then spins) and a minimal `printf` routed through
  the same MMIO. vsim's interconnect snoops the region and forwards
  bytes to stderr. Phase-1/3 in QEMU still uses libgloss_vh unchanged.
- Status: not-started

### F-T1.2 FP state not restored to vsim

- Trigger: Any workload using f0..f31 inside ROI.
- Breaks: QEMU1 plugin drains FPRs into `state.bin`. ResumeDriver does
  not write FPRs. vsim FPRs remain at RTL reset value. StateDrain
  passes through the stale QEMU1 snapshot to QEMU2 unchanged.
  FP computations in ROI silently corrupt register contents.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`,
  `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`
- Fix: Shipped in commit `59be1af` (2026-05-17). ResumeDriver writes
  f0..f31 via DM Abstract Commands after gating mstatus.FS;
  StateDrain.drain_with_passthrough freshly reads f0..f31 when
  HYBRID_FLAG_FP is set.
- Status: done

### F-T1.3 PMP state not restored to vsim

- Trigger: Any workload depending on PMP-enforced memory regions
  inside ROI.
- Breaks: Plugin drains pmpcfg[0..15] and pmpaddr[0..63] but
  ResumeDriver writes none. vsim sees zeroed PMP entries; any
  write-protect or execute-lock setup from QEMU1 evaporates.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`
- Fix: Mirror the FP pattern from F-T1.2. ResumeDriver writes
  pmpaddr[0..63] (CSRs 0x3B0..0x3EF) first, then pmpcfg[0..15] (CSRs
  0x3A0..0x3AF) via `write_csr_abstract_or_pb`; addr-before-cfg so
  lock bits apply last. StateDrain.drain_with_passthrough adds the
  mirror read loop. ABI fields already exist; no version bump.
- Status: not-started

### F-T1.4 Exception state lost across vsim ROI

- Trigger: Any trap (illegal-insn, page-fault, alignment, ebreak) fires
  inside ROI.
- Breaks: StateDrain returns the QEMU1 snapshot for mcause/mtval/mepc
  instead of vsim's current trap state. QEMU2 receives stale values;
  any post-handoff trap diagnostic logic is wrong.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` (passthrough path)
- Fix: In StateDrain.drain_with_passthrough, always freshly read
  mcause (0x342), mtval (0x343), mepc (0x341), and mstatus (0x300)
  via the existing read_64bit_register helper. Overwrite the
  pass-through values unconditionally; no flag gate needed.
- Status: not-started

### F-T1.5 LR/SC reservation not transferred

- Trigger: An lr.{w,d} retires before the trigger and the matching
  sc.{w,d} retires after - or any atomic across the handoff.
- Breaks: Reservation is not in the ABI struct, never round-trips.
  sc.* always fails on re-entry, so any `__atomic_compare_exchange`
  loop spins forever.
- Source: `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (missing field); resume/drain
  both
- Fix: Enforce trigger discipline rather than round-tripping the
  reservation. QEMU plugin checks `env->load_res.valid` at drain time
  and aborts with a clear diagnostic if a reservation is pending,
  asking the workload to place the trigger after a `fence rw,rw` or
  outside any lr/sc-bracketed region. Matches the existing "trigger
  is a sync point" assumption; no ABI bump.
- Status: not-started

## Tier 2 - Will fail when scaling

### F-T2.1 priv mode hardcoded to M

- Trigger: Linux/kernel workload running in S- or U-mode at the
  trigger boundary.
- Breaks: `state_drain.hpp:40` and `resume_driver.hpp:40` force
  `priv=3`; DCSR.prv never written. `dret` always resumes in M-mode.
  S/U code raises illegal-insn -> trap loop.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`,
  `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`
- Fix: Drain - replace `state.priv = 3` with a DCSR read (CSR 0x7B0,
  mask bits 1:0). Resume - before `request_resume()`, write DCSR.prv
  from state.priv via `write_csr_abstract_or_pb` so the subsequent
  `dret` restores the recorded mode. ABI `priv` field already exists.
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.2 Workload exceeding 128MB mmap

- Trigger: Heap, stack, or static data extending past the default 128MB
  shared mmap.
- Breaks: vsim's SimpleMemory bounds reject the access or silently
  truncate; QEMU sees MMIO instead of RAM. No startup-time bounds
  check on ELF segments against mmap size.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/platform/simple_memory.hpp`,
  orchestrator mmap sizing
- Fix: Orchestrator startup parses the ELF PHDRs; sums max(vaddr +
  memsz) across LOAD segments; aborts with a diagnostic if the value
  exceeds the mmap size. Expose mmap size via env var
  `HYBRID_MMAP_BYTES` (default 128MB) so large workloads opt in.
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.3 Multi-hart resume path

- Trigger: `_NDS_NHART=2` configuration; workload using hart 1, atomic
  fences, IPIs, or hart-to-hart sync.
- Breaks: ABI carries `hart_id` and per-hart arrays but ResumeDriver
  and StateDrain only handle hart 0. Resuming hart 1 from hart 0's
  state corrupts both.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/{resume_driver,state_drain}.hpp`,
  `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (thread selection)
- Fix: Wrap ResumeDriver/StateDrain in a per-hart loop. Before each
  pass, write DMCONTROL.hartsello (bits 16:25) to select the hart;
  re-poll allhalted, then run the existing single-hart code.
  qemu_handback prefixes each per-hart P-packet block with `Hg
  <thread-id>`. Gated by hart_count > 1 so single-hart path is
  unchanged.
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.4 mcycle/minstret monotonicity

- Trigger: Long-running workload; or any consumer reading these CSRs
  across the handoff.
- Breaks: No test asserts monotonicity. If the icount trigger is off
  by one, vsim's mcycle could be less than QEMU1's exit mcycle and
  nothing would catch it.
- Source: gap in test suite; ABI does carry mcycle/minstret
- Fix: Add a CTest case that captures QEMU1's drained mcycle/minstret
  from state.bin, then queries QEMU2's gdbstub for the same CSRs
  immediately on connect; assert strictly increasing. Wire into
  `run_e2e.sh` so every Tier-2 fixture exercises it.
- Status: not-started

### F-T2.5 MMIO inside ROI

- Trigger: Workload calls `putchar`, touches UART/PLIC, accesses ROM,
  or hits aliased memory inside ROI.
- Breaks: Phase-5 interconnect watch (`hybrid_plan.md` Section 5) was
  never implemented. Writes to MMIO ranges either vanish or hit
  SimpleMemory as if they were RAM. QEMU2 device state desyncs from
  vsim's view.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/platform/interconnect.hpp` (no watch);
  `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5
- Fix: Define a single allowlist of RAM + permitted-MMIO ranges in
  `src/platform/interconnect.hpp`. SimpleMemory::read/write checks
  every access against the allowlist; out-of-range aborts with a
  diagnostic logging the offending PC + address. Implements the
  Phase-5 interconnect watch.
- Status: not-started

## Tier 3 - Bug surfaces invisible to current suite

### F-T3.1 State-value opacity in M3-M7 demos

- Trigger: Any bug that copies the wrong register, byte-swaps, or
  truncates a 64-bit value during M3 (icount), M4 (BBV), M5
  (slice-consume), M6 (archive), or M7 (replay).
- Breaks: Demos `demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`,
  `demo_bbv_profile.sh`, `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`,
  `demo_archive_replay.sh`, `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` only assert exit
  code + PC range. A wrong register value passes all of them.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_*.sh`
- Fix: In each demo, after QEMU2 connects via gdbstub, issue a single
  `g` packet to read the full register file; parse and memcmp byte
  by byte against the corresponding fields in state.bin. Fail the
  demo on mismatch. ~30 lines of bash per demo via a shared helper.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.2 Untested trigger-mode combinations

- Trigger: csr-entry + icount-exit; slice-entry + pc-exit;
  BBV-emit with simultaneous icount; etc. ~32 entry/exit pairings
  exist; suite tests CSR+CSR and PC+PC only.
- Breaks: Plugin or vsim bug in a cross-mode path is invisible until
  hit in production.
- Source: gap in `run_e2e.sh` mode coverage
- Fix: Parameterize `run_e2e.sh` with separate `ENTRY_MODE` and
  `EXIT_MODE` env vars (default = MODE if unset). Add a top-level
  `matrix` mode that iterates the cross-product across one canonical
  fixture, skipping pairings the plugin rejects up front.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.3 Submodule version drift

- Trigger: Upstream QEMU 9.x changes TCG plugin ABI; vsim refactors
  the DM; SimPoint pinned to 17783e5 stays unmaintained.
- Breaks: Plugin silently produces wrong state, or fails to build,
  with no CI gate.
- Source: `.gitmodules`; no CI matrix against upstream snapshots
- Fix: Add `scripts/check_upstream_drift.sh` that, for each submodule,
  `git fetch`es the upstream remote and reports commits since the
  pinned SHA. Schedule a weekly CI job that runs the script and
  attaches the report to an auto-filed GitHub issue when non-empty.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.4 vlenb mismatch silent

- Trigger: Fixture built with `--march=rv64gcv` for VLEN=512 (vlenb=64)
  runs on a vsim configured for VLEN=256 (vlenb=32) - or vice versa.
- Breaks: No cross-check that `vlenb_qemu == vlenb_vsim`. ResumeDriver
  reads only `vlenb` bytes of V-reg payload; upper bytes left as
  uninitialized scratch. Garbage in V regs.
- Source: gap in `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` and ABI
  validator
- Fix: At handoff entry, before any V transport, ResumeDriver reads
  the hart's `vlenb` CSR (0xC22) via Abstract Command and compares
  against `state.vlenb`. Mismatch aborts with a clear error naming
  both values. Same check on qemu_handback ingress side.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.5 Failure-injection holes

- Trigger: Partial state drain mid-handoff; SIGKILL; disk full; mmap
  truncated to wrong size; corrupt magic with valid struct size.
- Breaks: Existing negative drivers cover only 4 scenarios
  (corrupt-state-short-read, no-qemu-binary, gdbstub-timeout,
  pty-handback). Subtle resource-exhaustion or race-condition paths
  unguarded.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_{corrupt_state,no_qemu_binary,gdbstub_timeout,pty_handback}.sh`
- Fix: Add three negative drivers: `rt_fault_partial_drain.sh`
  (truncate state.bin to half its size mid-handoff),
  `rt_fault_disk_full.sh` (run on a size-capped tmpfs smaller than
  state size), `rt_fault_mmap_resize.sh` (truncate the shared mmap
  while vsim is mid-execution). Each asserts a clean diagnostic exit.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.6 Determinism across re-runs

- Trigger: Run the same ELF twice; diff the drained state files.
- Breaks: No guarantee of byte-identical output. `reserved[]` bytes,
  CSR drain order, and FP rounding sources could vary. Any test using
  exact-equality assertion is flaky.
- Source: `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` drain order; ABI reserved
  bytes
- Fix: Add an e2e test that runs the same fixture twice and asserts
  `sha256sum state1.bin == sha256sum state2.bin`. Audit drain path:
  zero-initialise `reserved[]` explicitly in the plugin and in
  StateDrain; serialise CSR writes in canonical CSR-number order.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.7 Self-modifying code

- Trigger: JIT or workload writes to .text inside ROI.
- Breaks: Shared mmap is live so the write is visible immediately,
  but QEMU1 has cached the old insn in TCG and vsim's icache starts
  cold. Same binary -> different trace.
- Source: documented out-of-scope in `hybrid_plan.md` Section 2.5;
  not detected
- Fix: At orchestrator startup, parse the ELF for `.text` PHDRs and
  call `mprotect(..., PROT_READ)` on the corresponding mmap pages.
  Any write fault becomes a clean SIGSEGV with a diagnostic naming
  the offending PC. SMC stays out-of-scope but is now detected
  instead of silently corrupting traces.
- Status: not-started

### F-T3.8 GDB handshake timeout / zombie QEMU2

- Trigger: QEMU2 launches but its gdbstub does not respond within 5s,
  or responds then hangs mid-P-packet.
- Breaks: `qemu_handback::connect_loopback` may time out but the
  spawned QEMU2 child can be left as a zombie if the orchestrator
  has no SIGALRM watchdog.
- Source: `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`
- Fix: In `connect_loopback`, install a `setitimer`-based watchdog
  before the gdb handshake. On SIGALRM, `kill(child_pid, SIGKILL)`
  then `waitpid(child_pid, ..., 0)` to reap, then throw. RAII guard
  ensures reap happens on any throw path.
- Status: not-started

## Highest-leverage next moves

1. **Add register-readback assertions to demos T3-T7** - cheapest fix
   for F-T3.1. Single gdbstub `g` packet at QEMU2 entry, memcmp
   against the drained `state.bin`.
2. **Land `docs/v_csr_plan.md`** - already a 9-step TDD sequence;
   partially addresses F-T1.2 and F-T1.3 by establishing the
   ResumeDriver pattern for Tier-A and Tier-B CSRs.
3. **Promote `hybrid_plan.md` Phase 5 hardening to a tracked ROADMAP** -
   covers F-T1.2, F-T1.3, F-T1.4, F-T1.5, F-T2.1, F-T2.3, F-T2.5 in
   one scoped effort. Currently never scheduled.

lastPrompt: TODO.md : Semihosting inside ROI is it possible to add the same semihosting features inside the verilator so that we could call printf ...etc

input.old_string:
- Fix: Drop libgloss_vh inside ROI. Compile a custom crt0 + handler.c
  that provides a local `_exit` (writes status word to an MMIO debug
  console region, then spins) and a minimal `printf` routed through
  the same MMIO. vsim's interconnect snoops the region and forwards
  bytes to stderr. Phase-1/3 in QEMU still uses libgloss_vh unchanged.
input.new_string:
- Fix: Add a host-side semihosting handler inside vsim so libgloss_vh
  keeps working unchanged across the ROI. Pre-scan the ELF for the
  magic triple `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7` (same pattern
  template as `csr_trigger.hpp:37`); map each ebreak PC to a new
  `HYBRID_SEMIHOST_CALL` trigger kind. HandoffController dispatches to
  a `SemihostHandler` that: halts the hart via DMI, reads `a0` (call
  number) + `a1` (arg block ptr) using the existing
  `read_64bit_register` helper, services SYS_WRITE/SYS_WRITE0/
  SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED on the host (memory reads via the shared mmap
  already mapped by orchestrator), writes the return value back into
  `a0`, advances DPC by 12 bytes (past the full triple, not just
  ebreak), then resumes. No fixture / libgloss changes needed.

content.text:
# /loop — schedule a recurring or self-paced prompt

Parse the input below into `[interval] <prompt…>` and schedule it.

## Parsing (in priority order)

1. **Leading token**: if the first whitespace-delimited token matches `^\d+[smhd]$` (e.g. `5m`, `2h`), that's the interval; the rest is the prompt.
2. **Trailing "every" clause**: otherwise, if the input ends with `every <N><unit>` or `every <N> <unit-word>` (e.g. `every 20m`, `every 5 minutes`, `every 2 hours`), extract that as the interval and strip it from the prompt. Only match when what follows "every" is a time expression — `check every PR` has no interval.
3. **No interval**: otherwise, the entire input is the prompt and you'll self-pace dynamically (see "Dynamic mode" below).

If the resulting prompt is empty, show usage `/loop [interval] <prompt>` and stop.

Examples:
- `5m /babysit-prs` → interval `5m`, prompt `/babysit-prs` (rule 1)
- `check the deploy every 20m` → interval `20m`, prompt `check the deploy` (rule 2)
- `run tests every 5 minutes` → interval `5m`, prompt `run tests` (rule 2)
- `check the deploy` → no interval → dynamic mode, prompt `check the deploy` (rule 3)
- `check every PR` → no interval → dynamic mode, prompt `check every PR` (rule 3 — "every" not followed by time)
- `5m` → empty prompt → show usage

## Offer cloud first

Before any scheduling step, check whether EITHER is true:
- the parsed interval (rule 1 or 2) is **≥60 minutes**, or
- regardless of which rule matched, the original input uses daily phrasing ("every morning", "daily", "every day", "each night", "every weekday")

If either is true, call AskUserQuestion first:
- `question`: "This loop stops when you close this session. Set it up as a cloud schedule instead so it keeps running?"
- `header`: "Schedule"
- `options`: `[{label: "Cloud schedule (recommended)", description: "Runs in Anthropic's cloud even after you close this session"}, {label: "This session only", description: "Runs in this terminal until you exit"}]`

If they pick **Cloud schedule**: do NOT call CronCreate. Invoke the `schedule` skill directly via the Skill tool with `args` set to their original input verbatim (e.g. `Skill({skill: "schedule", args: "every morning tell me a joke"})`), then follow that skill's instructions to completion. Do NOT tell the user to run /schedule themselves. **Then stop — do not continue to any section below** (no CronCreate, no ScheduleWakeup, no "execute the prompt now").
If they pick **This session only**:
- If the trigger was a parsed ≥60-minute interval (rule 1 or 2): continue below with that interval.
- If the trigger was daily phrasing only (rule 3, no parsed interval): do NOT call CronCreate. Explain that a daily-cadence loop won't fire before this session closes, so there's nothing useful to schedule locally — suggest they either pick Cloud schedule, or re-run `/loop` with an explicit shorter interval (e.g. `/loop 1h <prompt>`) if they want a session loop. Then stop.
If neither trigger condition was met: continue below.

## Fixed-interval mode (rules 1 and 2)

Convert the interval to a cron expression:

| Interval pattern      | Cron expression     | Notes                                    |
|-----------------------|---------------------|------------------------------------------|
| `Nm` where N ≤ 59   | `*/N * * * *`     | every N minutes                          |
| `Nm` where N ≥ 60   | `0 */H * * *`     | round to hours (H = N/60, must divide 24)|
| `Nh` where N ≤ 23   | `0 */N * * *`     | every N hours                            |
| `Nd`                | `0 0 */N * *`     | every N days at midnight local           |
| `Ns`                | treat as `ceil(N/60)m` | cron minimum granularity is 1 minute  |

**If the interval doesn't cleanly divide its unit** (e.g. `7m` → `*/7 * * * *` gives uneven gaps at :56→:00; `90m` → 1.5h which cron can't express), pick the nearest clean interval and tell the user what you rounded to before scheduling.

Then:
1. Call CronCreate with: `cron` (the expression above), `prompt` (the parsed prompt verbatim), `recurring: true`.
2. Briefly confirm: what's scheduled, the cron expression, the human-readable cadence, that recurring tasks auto-expire after 7 days, and that the user can cancel sooner with CronDelete (include the job ID). Only if you did NOT show the cloud-offer AskUserQuestion above (i.e., neither trigger condition applied), end the confirmation with this exact line on its own, italicized: `_Runs until you close this session · For durable cloud-based loops, use /schedule_`. If the user already answered that question, omit this line.
3. **Then immediately execute the parsed prompt now** — don't wait for the first cron fire. If it's a slash command, invoke it via the Skill tool; otherwise act on it directly.

## Dynamic mode (rule 3 — no interval)

The user wants you to self-pace. Decide what makes the next iteration worth running — a passage of time, or an observable event.

1. **Run the parsed prompt now.** If it's a slash command, invoke it via the Skill tool; otherwise act on it directly.
2. **If the next run is gated on an event** (CI finishing, a log line matching, a file changing, a PR comment) and no Monitor is already running for it: arm one now with `persistent: true`. Its events arrive as `<task-notification>` messages and wake this loop immediately — you do not wait for the ScheduleWakeup deadline. Arm once; on later iterations call TaskList first and skip this step if a monitor is already running.
3. **At the end of this turn, call ScheduleWakeup** with:
   - `delaySeconds`: with a Monitor armed this is the **fallback heartbeat** — how long to wait if no event fires (lean 1200–1800s; idle ticks past the 5-minute cache window are pure overhead). Without a Monitor this is the cadence — pick based on what you observed. Read the tool's own description for cache-aware delay guidance.
   - `reason`: one short sentence on why you picked that delay.
   - `prompt`: the full original /loop input verbatim, prefixed with `/loop ` so the next firing re-enters this skill and continues the loop. For example, if the user typed `/loop check the deploy`, pass `/loop check the deploy` as the prompt.
4. **If you were woken by a `<task-notification>`** rather than this prompt: handle the event in the context of the loop task, then call ScheduleWakeup again with the same `prompt` and the same 1200–1800s `delaySeconds` from step 3 — the Monitor remains the wake signal; this only resets the safety net.
5. **To stop the loop**, omit the ScheduleWakeup call and TaskStop any Monitor you armed (use TaskList to find the task ID if it is no longer in context).
6. Briefly confirm: that you're self-pacing, whether a Monitor is the primary wake signal, that you ran the task now, and what fallback delay you picked.

message.content: I don't fully understand. Does that mean that vsim and QEMU will have the same behavior for all system calls?

content.text:
No. Only for the stateless syscalls. Stateful ones diverge across the QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 boundary.

**Will match** (no state outlives the syscall):
- `SYS_WRITE`/`SYS_WRITE0` (bytes -> stderr): each call is independent. Same string goes to the same stderr.
- `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`: terminal call, no surviving state.
- Single-shot reads from stdin if we route them to the same TTY.

**Will diverge**:
- **File I/O** (`SYS_OPEN`/`SYS_READ`/`SYS_WRITE` on a file/`SYS_CLOSE`): QEMU keeps its own fd table; vsim would keep a separate one. If the ROI opens fd 7 and exits without closing, QEMU2 has no fd 7 - the next read crashes.
- **`SYS_TIME` / `SYS_CLOCK`**: QEMU returns wall-clock seconds; vsim is cycle-accurate. A program calling `time()` in phase 1 then again in phase 2 sees a discontinuity.
- **`SYS_GET_CMDLINE`**: returns argv. vsim would have to mirror what QEMU was launched with.
- **`SYS_HEAPINFO`**: returns heap base/limit. Must match the mmap layout vsim already shares with QEMU - usually fine, but a divergence here is silent corruption.
- **`SYS_ERRNO`**: per-fd errno state, same problem as file I/O.

The clean rule remains: **don't issue stateful syscalls inside the ROI**. The handler in vsim is best scoped to the stateless subset (write/exit/maybe heapinfo if we mirror QEMU exactly), and stateful calls should still be gated to phases 1 and 3. The fixture build can lint for this with `nm | grep`-style checks on the ROI section.

Want me to narrow the Fix proposal in TODO.md to reflect this scope (stateless-only + lint gate)?

message.content: explain F-T1.5

content.text:
F-T1.5 is about RISC-V's atomic instruction pair: `lr.w/lr.d` (load-reserved) and `sc.w/sc.d` (store-conditional).

**How lr/sc works normally**:
1. `lr.d a0, (a1)` - read from address `a1` into `a0`, AND tell the CPU "I'm reserving this address"
2. ...do some work...
3. `sc.d t0, a2, (a1)` - try to write `a2` to address `a1`. Only succeeds (`t0=0`) if nobody else wrote to that address since the `lr`. If somebody did, the store fails (`t0=1`) and the program retries from step 1.

This is how C's `__atomic_compare_exchange` is built on RISC-V.

**The problem (F-T1.5)**:
The "reservation" is hidden CPU state - it's not a register, not a CSR, not in memory. It lives inside the CPU model as a flag (`env->load_res.valid` in QEMU).

If the handoff happens between `lr` and `sc`:
1. QEMU1 runs `lr.d`, sets its reservation flag.
2. Trigger fires. QEMU1 drains GPRs/CSRs/V state to `state.bin`. **Reservation flag is not saved** - `hybrid_state_v1` has no field for it.
3. vsim starts. Reservation flag is zero (cold start).
4. vsim runs `sc.d` - fails because no reservation.
5. The C program sees the cmpxchg failed, loops back to `lr`. But the `lr` is in phase 1 code, not phase 2. So the loop spins on `sc` failing forever.

**The fix**:
Two options:
- Hard option: add a `lr_addr` + `lr_valid` field to the ABI, drain it in QEMU plugin, restore it in ResumeDriver. Requires ABI version bump and DM access to the CPU's hidden reservation flag (which may not exist).
- Cheap option (what I documented): **don't allow the trigger to land between lr and sc**. At drain time, the QEMU plugin checks `env->load_res.valid`. If true, it aborts with a clear error: "trigger landed inside an atomic region, place it after a `fence rw,rw` or outside the lr/sc pair".

The cheap option matches the existing rule that "the trigger is a sync point" (workloads already cannot place the trigger in the middle of a multi-instruction operation that needs context preserved). No ABI bump. No DM access to hidden state. The workload author gets a clear message instead of a silent hang.

message.content: explain F-T2.4

content.text:
F-T2.4 is about two hardware counters: `mcycle` and `minstret`.

**What they are**:
- `mcycle` = total CPU cycles since the CPU started.
- `minstret` = total instructions retired since the CPU started.

Both are CSRs that monotonically **increase**. They should never go backwards. Profilers, kernel schedulers, and benchmarks all depend on this rule.

**The hybrid pipeline view**:
```
QEMU1 (mcycle 0 -> 1000)
   ↓ drain into state.bin at mcycle=1000
vsim (continues 1000 -> 1500)
   ↓ drain into state.bin at mcycle=1500
QEMU2 (continues 1500 -> ...)
```

Across the whole pipeline, mcycle/minstret must be monotonically non-decreasing. If at any boundary the next phase's counter is **less than** the previous phase's final value, a program reading them sees time going backwards.

**Where it could break**:
- Off-by-one in the icount trigger: QEMU1 drains at instruction N, but the plugin recorded `minstret = N-1`. vsim resumes thinking it's at N-1 and re-executes one instruction.
- vsim's reset value of mcycle gets used instead of restoring the drained value.
- QEMU2 restores via gdbstub but the gdbstub doesn't accept writes to read-only-mirror CSRs (`mcycle` has special handling in some QEMU versions).
- An entire phase's contribution gets lost (vsim ran 500 cycles, but they never get added because vsim's counter starts from zero on resume).

Right now, **no test catches any of these**. A regression would silently produce wrong values; only a workload that reads the counter would notice.

**The fix**:
A simple integration test:
1. Run a fixture. Capture `state.bin` from QEMU1's drain. Read out `mcycle` and `minstret` fields.
2. Let vsim run. Capture `state.bin` after vsim drain. Read the same fields.
3. After QEMU2 starts, connect to its gdbstub and read the same CSRs.
4. Assert: `qemu1_mcycle <= vsim_mcycle <= qemu2_mcycle` (and same for minstret).

Wire into `run_e2e.sh` so every Tier-2 fixture runs the check. ~50 lines of bash + a tiny gdb script. ABI already carries the fields - no code change to the handoff path, just observability that today's invisible bug becomes a CI failure.

content.text:
The plan file is up to date. Let me answer your F-T2.4 question.

"It will fail" is broader than just one bug - it covers any of these silent-data-corruption cases. The most concrete one is **right now, today**:

**Check the current restore/drain code**:
- `resume_driver.hpp:78-109` writes mstatus, FPRs, V-CSRs, GPRs, DPC, mtvec. **It does NOT write mcycle or minstret** into vsim.
- `state_drain.hpp:55-78` `drain_with_passthrough` freshly reads GPRs, PC, FPRs, V-CSRs. **It does NOT read mcycle or minstret** from vsim - it just passes through whatever QEMU1 drained.

So the actual current behavior:
```
QEMU1 mcycle = 1000  --drain--> state.bin mcycle = 1000
vsim runs 500 cycles internally (its own local counter)
StateDrain --passthrough--> state.bin mcycle = 1000 (unchanged!)
QEMU2 restores mcycle = 1000
```

**Result**: a C program calling `csrr a0, mcycle` before the ROI gets 1000; calling it after the ROI gets 1000. The 500 cycles vsim spent are **gone**. The cycle counter says no time passed in vsim.

That's failure mode #1: vsim's contribution is silently lost. A monotonicity test wouldn't catch this alone (1000 == 1000 is still monotonic), but a "vsim contributed something" test would.

**The other scenarios** (off-by-one, reset value, gdbstub mirror CSR) are bugs that **could** exist if someone adds mcycle restore logic. The test would catch them at that point too. So the test serves two purposes:
1. Today: catches the silently-lost vsim cycles.
2. After future code adds mcycle restore: catches any monotonicity regression introduced by that code.

Report these specific facts under 500 words, with file:line references for each:

1. **State file lifecycle**: Looking at `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_shared_mem.sh` (or whatever the Tier-2 harness is called), where is the state.bin written by QEMU1, where is the post-vsim state.bin written, and are BOTH preserved on disk after the test or does one overwrite the other? I need to read both `mcycle` from QEMU1's drain AND `mcycle` from vsim's drain. If they get overwritten, my test cannot compare them.

2. **State binary offset for mcycle/minstret**: Search for `state_abi_check.h` (probably at `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h`) and report the exact byte offsets of `mcycle` and `minstret` fields. Also report what assertion macro the file uses (so I know it'll catch a layout drift).

3. **Bash test helper patterns**: Look at existing demos like `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo_csr_oneshot.sh` (or similar). How do they parse fields out of state.bin? Is there a helper script for byte-extraction (like `od -An -tu8 -j$OFFSET -N8`)? Anything reusable I should import?

4. **CTest registration**: Find `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`. Show me the exact pattern for registering a new bash-based e2e test (a 5-line snippet plus the file:line where it lives). Tell me which target name + label convention to use.

5. **gdbstub readout in tests**: Looking at any existing demo that connects to QEMU2's gdbstub after handoff (e.g., demos that check state in QEMU2), how is the gdb session driven? Is it via `gdb -batch -ex 'target remote ...' -ex 'p $mcycle'`, or a helper script, or via Python? Show me the pattern.

6. **vsim mcycle access**: Looking at `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` and `resume_driver.hpp` (already in this session's context), is `mcycle` (CSR 0xB00) writable / readable via DM Abstract Commands on Andes ax45mpv? Search for any test or doc that exercises DM access to performance counters. (If unclear from the code, just note "unknown - will discover at runtime".)

7. **Magic CSR offset reference**: Confirm the line numbers in state_abi.h where mcycle and minstret are defined. (I already have it open: lines 61-62 with `uint64_t mcycle; uint64_t minstret;`. Verify.)

Just report findings - I'll do the implementation. No code suggestions, no design choices.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me compile all the facts:

## Report: F-T2.4 mcycle/minstret Monotonicity Test - Reconnaissance

**1. State File Lifecycle (rt_shared_mem.sh)**

In `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh` lines 66-70:
- `STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)` - created at line 67
- `STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)` - created at line 68
- Both are passed to subprocesses: `outfile=$STATE_QEMU` to QEMU1 (line 74), `--handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM"` to vsim (line 118)
- `trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT` - both are deleted only at script exit (line 70)
- **Verdict**: BOTH state files persist on disk throughout the test until cleanup. Your test can read `STATE_QEMU` (QEMU1's mcycle) before vsim runs, then read `STATE_VSIM` (vsim's mcycle) after vsim completes, then query QEMU2's gdbstub for the final mcycle—all three drains are available for comparison.

**2. State Binary Offsets for mcycle/minstret**

From `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h`:
- `static_assert(offsetof(struct hybrid_state_v1, mcycle)   == 624,  "mcycle@624");` - line 34
- `static_assert(offsetof(struct hybrid_state_v1, minstret) == 632,  "minstret@632");` - line 35
- Assertion macro: `static_assert(condition, message)` (C++20) / `_Static_assert` (C11) defined at lines 9-12
- Will catch layout drift at compile-time across all three components (QEMU plugin, vsim orchestrator, tests).

**3. Bash Test Helper Patterns**

In `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/probe_vcsr_drain.sh` lines 44-48:
```bash
read_u64_le() {
  local off=$1
  od -A n -j "$off" -N 8 -t u8 "$STATE" | tr -d ' \n'
}
```
- Uses `od -j $offset -N 8 -t u8 $file` to extract 8-byte little-endian uint64 at byte offset
- Output is plain decimal (u8 = unsigned 8-bit per byte, reconstructed as decimal by `od`)
- Wrapper returns a single decimal number ready for numeric comparison
- Applied at lines 50-52 for vxsat/vxrm/vcsr verification
- **Reusable**: identical pattern works for mcycle (offset 624) and minstret (offset 632).

**4. CTest Registration Pattern**

From `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` lines 223-244 (Tier-2 Fixtures):
```cmake
add_test(
  NAME test_e2e_${fname}
  COMMAND bash -c
    "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
)
set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
  SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
  LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
  COST ${tcost}
)
```
- New test name: `test_e2e_rt_mcycle_monotonicity` (convention: lowercase fixture name in test ID)
- If standalone driver (new .sh script), register as: `NAME test_e2e_rt_mcycle_monotonicity COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_mcycle_monotonicity.sh`
- If wrapper harness (adding logic to rt_shared_mem.sh), parameterize via env var then reuse existing fixture registration
- Labels: add `"hybrid-e2e-mcycle"` to group related tests separately from the round-trip suites
- SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77 exempts missing artifacts (QEMU/plugin/ELF) from CI failure.

**5. gdbstub Readout in Tests**

The codebase **reads** state from QEMU2 via gdbstub internally (in `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` lines 280-331): the `apply_state()` function uses `send_command(gdbstub::encode_p_body(...))` to **write** P packets. **No read-register helper exists in the hybrid codebase.**

However, two viable patterns emerge:
- **(A) Shell + gdb batch**: `gdb -batch -ex 'target remote :PORT' -ex 'p $mcycle' -ex 'p $minstret'` piped to extract values (simplest for a .sh test)
- **(B) Python + pygdbmi** (if available in container): parse gdbstub responses programmatically (more robust but requires dependency)
- **Current state**: rt_shared_mem.sh spawns QEMU2 via C++ orchestrator in vsim's main.cpp, which internally sends P packets but does NOT read back. A test harness would need its own gdb session or a C++ unit test with a recording mock.

**6. vsim mcycle/minstret Access via DM Abstract Commands**

From `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`:
- Lines 138-150: `write_csr_abstract_or_pb()` uses **Abstract Commands first** (line 146), then **Program Buffer fallback** if cmderr is set (lines 147-150)
- Lines 99-101: V CSRs (vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr) use this pattern; mcycle/minstret are not explicitly listed
- **Status**: The infrastructure exists to read mcycle/minstret via Abstract Commands (same as any other CSR). StateDrain::read_64bit_register() at line 81 performs the DM read; it can target any CSR number. No test or code path currently reads mcycle/minstret via DM.
- **Andes ax45mpv Performance Counter Support**: Unknown from code inspection. CSR 0xB00 (mcycle) and 0xB02 (minstret) are standard RV64 M-mode CSRs; typically readable but **writability** and **DM Abstract access** are implementation-specific. Discovery at runtime is safest (Abstract Command returns cmderr if unsupported).

**7. Magic CSR Offset Reference**

From `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/include/hybrid/state_abi.h`:
- Line 61: `uint64_t mcycle;`
- Line 62: `uint64_t minstret;`
- Confirmed. struct layout guarantees offset 624 and 632 (validated by state_abi_check.h assertions).

---

All facts collected. No code suggestions per your instructions. Ready for implementation.

## Report: F-T2.4 mcycle/minstret Monotonicity Test - Reconnaissance

**1. State File Lifecycle (rt_shared_mem.sh)**

In `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh` lines 66-70:
- `STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)` - created at line 67
- `STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)` - created at line 68
- Both are passed to subprocesses: `outfile=$STATE_QEMU` to QEMU1 (line 74), `--handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM"` to vsim (line 118)
- `trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT` - both are deleted only at script exit (line 70)
- **Verdict**: BOTH state files persist on disk throughout the test until cleanup. Your test can read `STATE_QEMU` (QEMU1's mcycle) before vsim runs, then read `STATE_VSIM` (vsim's mcycle) after vsim completes, then query QEMU2's gdbstub for the final mcycle—all three drains are available for comparison.

**2. State Binary Offsets for mcycle/minstret**

From `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h`:
- `static_assert(offsetof(struct hybrid_state_v1, mcycle)   == 624,  "mcycle@624");` - line 34
- `static_assert(offsetof(struct hybrid_state_v1, minstret) == 632,  "minstret@632");` - line 35
- Assertion macro: `static_assert(condition, message)` (C++20) / `_Static_assert` (C11) defined at lines 9-12
- Will catch layout drift at compile-time across all three components (QEMU plugin, vsim orchestrator, tests).

**3. Bash Test Helper Patterns**

In `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/probe_vcsr_drain.sh` lines 44-48:
```bash
read_u64_le() {
  local off=$1
  od -A n -j "$off" -N 8 -t u8 "$STATE" | tr -d ' \n'
}
```
- Uses `od -j $offset -N 8 -t u8 $file` to extract 8-byte little-endian uint64 at byte offset
- Output is plain decimal (u8 = unsigned 8-bit per byte, reconstructed as decimal by `od`)
- Wrapper returns a single decimal number ready for numeric comparison
- Applied at lines 50-52 for vxsat/vxrm/vcsr verification
- **Reusable**: identical pattern works for mcycle (offset 624) and minstret (offset 632).

**4. CTest Registration Pattern**

From `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` lines 223-244 (Tier-2 Fixtures):
```cmake
add_test(
  NAME test_e2e_${fname}
  COMMAND bash -c
    "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
)
set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
  SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
  LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
  COST ${tcost}
)
```
- New test name: `test_e2e_rt_mcycle_monotonicity` (convention: lowercase fixture name in test ID)
- If standalone driver (new .sh script), register as: `NAME test_e2e_rt_mcycle_monotonicity COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_mcycle_monotonicity.sh`
- If wrapper harness (adding logic to rt_shared_mem.sh), parameterize via env var then reuse existing fixture registration
- Labels: add `"hybrid-e2e-mcycle"` to group related tests separately from the round-trip suites
- SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77 exempts missing artifacts (QEMU/plugin/ELF) from CI failure.

**5. gdbstub Readout in Tests**

The codebase **reads** state from QEMU2 via gdbstub internally (in `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` lines 280-331): the `apply_state()` function uses `send_command(gdbstub::encode_p_body(...))` to **write** P packets. **No read-register helper exists in the hybrid codebase.**

However, two viable patterns emerge:
- **(A) Shell + gdb batch**: `gdb -batch -ex 'target remote :PORT' -ex 'p $mcycle' -ex 'p $minstret'` piped to extract values (simplest for a .sh test)
- **(B) Python + pygdbmi** (if available in container): parse gdbstub responses programmatically (more robust but requires dependency)
- **Current state**: rt_shared_mem.sh spawns QEMU2 via C++ orchestrator in vsim's main.cpp, which internally sends P packets but does NOT read back. A test harness would need its own gdb session or a C++ unit test with a recording mock.

**6. vsim mcycle/minstret Access via DM Abstract Commands**

From `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`:
- Lines 138-150: `write_csr_abstract_or_pb()` uses **Abstract Commands first** (line 146), then **Program Buffer fallback** if cmderr is set (lines 147-150)
- Lines 99-101: V CSRs (vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr) use this pattern; mcycle/minstret are not explicitly listed
- **Status**: The infrastructure exists to read mcycle/minstret via Abstract Commands (same as any other CSR). StateDrain::read_64bit_register() at line 81 performs the DM read; it can target any CSR number. No test or code path currently reads mcycle/minstret via DM.
- **Andes ax45mpv Performance Counter Support**: Unknown from code inspection. CSR 0xB00 (mcycle) and 0xB02 (minstret) are standard RV64 M-mode CSRs; typically readable but **writability** and **DM Abstract access** are implementation-specific. Discovery at runtime is safest (Abstract Command returns cmderr if unsupported).

**7. Magic CSR Offset Reference**

From `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/include/hybrid/state_abi.h`:
- Line 61: `uint64_t mcycle;`
- Line 62: `uint64_t minstret;`
- Confirmed. struct layout guarantees offset 624 and 632 (validated by state_abi_check.h assertions).

---

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

200	    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
201	    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
202	    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
203	    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
204	    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
205	    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
206	    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
207	  )
208	  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
209	    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
210	    list(GET parts 0 fname)
211	    list(GET parts 1 erc)
212	    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
213	    set(timeout_env "")
214	    set(tcost 60)
215	    if(nparts GREATER 2)
216	      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
217	      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
218	      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
219	      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
220	      set(tcost ${tsec})
221	    endif()
222	    # CSR variant.
223	    add_test(
224	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
225	      COMMAND bash -c
226	        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
227	    )
228	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
229	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
230	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
231	      COST ${tcost}
232	    )
233	    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
234	    add_test(
235	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
236	      COMMAND bash -c
237	        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
238	    )
239	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
240	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
241	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
242	      COST ${tcost}
243	    )
244	  endforeach()
245	
246	  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
247	  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
248	  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
249	  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
250	  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
251	  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
252	    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
253	      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)

256	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
257	    )
258	  endforeach()

260	  # Contract test: QEMU plugin exits 200 on drain (matches vsim's

262	  # exit(0), which would make workload-completion indistinguishable from

file.content:
# V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Contract test: QEMU plugin exits 200 on drain (matches vsim's
  # kExitHandoffToQemu). Guards the F1 rebrand from regressing back to
  # exit(0), which would make workload-completion indistinguishable from
  # a drained handoff under the orchestrator.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_drain_exit_code
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_drain_exit_code PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F5: plugin icount-trigger mode. Runs a CSR-less spin fixture with
  # icount=N; asserts drain at exit 200 and minstret >= N.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_icount_trigger
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_icount_trigger PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F7: plugin BBV-emit mode (SimPoint profile). Runs the CSR-less spin
  # with bbv_out= + slice= + icount=; asserts the .bb file has the
  # expected row count and is consumable by tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_bbv_emit
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_bbv_emit PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F11: plugin BBV slice-consume mode. Runs the CSR-less spin with
  # slice= + slice_at=K + icount=fallback; asserts drain at the end of
  # slice index K (0-based), before the icount fallback fires.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_phase_consume
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_phase_consume.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_phase_consume PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"

fi

# F-T2.4: mcycle/minstret must strictly increase from QEMU1's drain to
# vsim's drain. Offsets are locked by state_abi_check.h (mcycle@624,
# minstret@632). Opt-in via env var so existing Tier-2 round-trips stay
# focused on memory survival.
if [ "${CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC:-0}" = "1" ]; then
  read_u64_le() { od -A n -j "$1" -N 8 -t u8 "$2" | tr -d ' \n'; }
  Q1_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le 624 "$STATE_QEMU")
  Q1_MINST=$(read_u64_le 632 "$STATE_QEMU")
  V_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le 624 "$STATE_VSIM")
  V_MINST=$(read_u64_le 632 "$STATE_VSIM")
  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] qemu1: mcycle=$Q1_MCYCLE minstret=$Q1_MINST"
  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] vsim:  mcycle=$V_MCYCLE minstret=$V_MINST"
  if [ "$V_MCYCLE" -le "$Q1_MCYCLE" ]; then
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: mcycle not strictly increasing across vsim"
    echo "  qemu1=$Q1_MCYCLE vsim=$V_MCYCLE"
    exit 1
  fi
  if [ "$V_MINST" -le "$Q1_MINST" ]; then
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: minstret not strictly increasing across vsim"
    echo "  qemu1=$Q1_MINST vsim=$V_MINST"
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] PASS: strictly increasing"
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

+    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: minstret not strictly increasing across vsim"

150	    # tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
151	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
152	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
153	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
154	      COST 30
155	    )
156	    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
157	    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
158	    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
159	    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
160	    add_test(
161	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
162	      COMMAND bash -c
163	        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
164	    )
165	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
166	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
167	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
168	      COST 30
169	    )
170	  endforeach()
171	
172	  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
173	  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
174	  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
175	  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
176	  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
177	  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
178	  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
179	    "rt_shared_mem:0"
180	    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
181	    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
182	    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
183	    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
184	    "rt_c_hello:0"
185	    "rt_c_printf:0"
186	    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
187	    # Regression for the "no redundant ELF reload" rule: .bss survival
188	    # across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 without users hand-allocating fixed

195	    # picked V register; QEMU2 stores y and scalar-verifies. V regfile
196	    # transport rides on the save-all / reload-all helpers in

file.content:
# tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST 30
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST 30
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # Regression for the "no redundant ELF reload" rule: .bss survival
    # across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 without users hand-allocating fixed
    # addresses. Fails today because vsim's elf_loader and QEMU 2's
    # -kernel both zero-fill the .bss tail of every PT_LOAD.
    "rt_c_static_buf:0"
    # 1D 3-tap FIR convolution as a real RVV use case spanning the
    # handoff: QEMU1 generates x via intrinsics; vsim applies the kernel
    # (vslide1up / vslide1down + vadd) using x carried in its compiler-
    # picked V register; QEMU2 stores y and scalar-verifies. V regfile
    # transport rides on the save-all / reload-all helpers in
    # runtime/v_regfile_xfer.{h,c} so the fixture body is pure intrinsics
    # with no physical V register references.
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are

# Contract test: QEMU plugin exits 200 on drain (matches vsim's
  # kExitHandoffToQemu). Guards the F1 rebrand from regressing back to
  # exit(0), which would make workload-completion indistinguishable from
  # a drained handoff under the orchestrator.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_drain_exit_code
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh)
input.new_string:
# F-T2.4: mcycle/minstret monotonicity across the QEMU1 -> vsim handoff.
  # Reuses rt_shared_mem.sh with CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1; the harness reads
  # both drain files (mcycle@624, minstret@632) and asserts strictly
  # increasing. Fixture is rt_c_hello (cheap, runs the full Tier-2 path).
  add_test(
    NAME test_e2e_mcycle_monotonicity
    COMMAND bash -c
      "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0 CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1 bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  )
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_mcycle_monotonicity PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
    COST 30
  )

  # Contract test: QEMU plugin exits 200 on drain (matches vsim's

  # exit(0), which would make workload-completion indistinguishable from
  # a drained handoff under the orchestrator.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_drain_exit_code
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh)

diff --git a/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp b/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp
index f27ed73..571d755 100644
--- a/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp
+++ b/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp
@@ -35,12 +35,14 @@
 namespace hybrid
 {
 
-// Top of QEMU virt's default 128 MiB RAM (RAM_BASE 0x80000000 + 0x0FF0000
-// = 0x80FF0000). Below the C-runtime stack at 0x80700000 only when the
-// runtime grows beyond default; rt_c.ld places _stack at 0x80700000 so
-// 0x80FF0000 sits well above it. Document the reservation in fixture
-// comments so future memory-layout changes don't collide.
-inline constexpr uint64_t kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000ull;
+// Reserved scratch slot inside the shared mmap. Sits well above the C
+// runtime's _stack (rt_c.ld places it at RAM_BASE + 0x700000), giving the
+// stack ~9 MiB of headroom before colliding with this 2 KiB region. On
+// `-M andes_ae350` RAM is at 0x00000000 and the GPA is 0x00FF0000; on
+// `-M virt` it would be 0x80FF0000. The chosen address is RAM_BASE +
+// 0xFF0000, an offset that has to match the `li t0, ...` constant in the
+// fixture's runtime/v_regfile_xfer.c.
+inline constexpr uint64_t kVScratchGpa = 0x00FF0000ull;
 
 // Total scratch bytes needed: 32 V regs × HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Sized to
 // the maximum supported VLEN; the actual per-reg byte count is `vlenb`.

content.content:
diff --git a/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh b/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
index 18f9759..69a2b69 100755
--- a/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
+++ b/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
@@ -92,11 +92,11 @@ echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
 set +e
 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
   -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
-  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
+  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
   -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
-  -bios none -nographic \
+  -nographic \
   -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
-  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
+  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
   -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
 QEMU1_RC=$?
 set -e
@@ -118,6 +118,7 @@ timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
   --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
   --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
   --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
+  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
   --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
   "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
 RC=$?
@@ -130,4 +131,29 @@ if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
   exit 1
 fi
 
+# F-T2.4: mcycle/minstret must strictly increase from QEMU1's drain to
+# vsim's drain. Offsets are locked by state_abi_check.h (mcycle@624,
+# minstret@632). Opt-in via env var so existing Tier-2 round-trips stay
+# focused on memory survival.
+if [ "${CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC:-0}" = "1" ]; then
+  read_u64_le() { od -A n -j "$1" -N 8 -t u8 "$2" | tr -d ' \n'; }
+  Q1_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le 624 "$STATE_QEMU")
+  Q1_MINST=$(read_u64_le 632 "$STATE_QEMU")
+  V_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le 624 "$STATE_VSIM")
+  V_MINST=$(read_u64_le 632 "$STATE_VSIM")
+  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] qemu1: mcycle=$Q1_MCYCLE minstret=$Q1_MINST"
+  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] vsim:  mcycle=$V_MCYCLE minstret=$V_MINST"
+  if [ "$V_MCYCLE" -le "$Q1_MCYCLE" ]; then
+    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: mcycle not strictly increasing across vsim"
+    echo "  qemu1=$Q1_MCYCLE vsim=$V_MCYCLE"
+    exit 1
+  fi
+  if [ "$V_MINST" -le "$Q1_MINST" ]; then
+    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: minstret not strictly increasing across vsim"
+    echo "  qemu1=$Q1_MINST vsim=$V_MINST"
+    exit 1
+  fi
+  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] PASS: strictly increasing"
+fi
+
 echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"
